note: v2: +37 AI-tell/copy checks from impeccable+unslop distillation. Stage counts: {"static-text":70,"static-dom":69,"llm-pass":29,"browser":37,"transcript":2}

checks.title: Forbidden AI-slop font as primary --font-body
checks.applies_when: CSS declares a `--font-body` custom property or a `body`/`html { font-family }` rule.
checks.spec: Parse `--font-body: <stack>;` and any `body`/`html { font-family: <stack> }`. Extract the FIRST (primary) family in the stack, strip quotes. Fail if it case-insensitively equals Inter, Roboto, Arial, or Helvetica, OR if the whole stack is only generic keywords (system-ui / sans-serif / serif) with no named webfont preceding them. Only the primary entry counts — these names as trailing fallbacks pass. Named aesthetics (Mono-Industrial/Nothing) already use their own allowed primaries, so this fires only when a banned family sits in the primary slot.
checks.fixture_violation: Page whose `--font-body: 'Inter', sans-serif;` puts Inter in the primary slot.
checks.fp_guards: Ignore Inter/Roboto/Arial/Helvetica when they appear only as later fallbacks in the stack, in a non-body role (--font-mono, headings), or inside <code>/prose discussing fonts.
checks.impl_hint: /--font-body\s*:\s*([^;]+)/i then split(',')[0].replace(/['"]/g,'').trim()

checks.title: Forbidden Tailwind-default violet/neon accent hexes
checks.applies_when: Any <style> block or inline style= attribute present.
checks.spec: Case-insensitively scan <style> blocks and inline `style=""` attributes (NOT text nodes/code) for hex 8b5cf6, 7c3aed, a78bfa (indigo/violet), d946ef (fuchsia), 06b6d4, f472b6 (cyan/pink neon) — with or without leading #, plus their rgb()/rgba() equivalents. Any match = fail.
checks.fixture_violation: Page CSS with `--accent: #8b5cf6;`.
checks.fp_guards: Scope strictly to style contexts, not prose/code that documents the hex; Mermaid themeVariables colours are covered separately by the mermaid family.
checks.impl_hint: /#?(8b5cf6|7c3aed|a78bfa|d946ef|06b6d4|f472b6)\b/i restricted to <style>…</style> and style="…" spans

checks.title: Gradient-clipped text on headings
checks.applies_when: CSS contains `background-clip: text` or `-webkit-background-clip: text`.
checks.spec: Find CSS rules that combine a `linear-gradient(`/`-webkit-linear-gradient(` background with `background-clip: text` (or `-webkit-background-clip: text`) and transparent/clipped text fill, on a heading-ish selector (h1–h4, .title, .headline, .hero-title, .display, .kicker). Any such rule = fail.
checks.fixture_violation: `h1{background:linear-gradient(...);-webkit-background-clip:text;color:transparent}`.
checks.fp_guards: Very specific combo; low FP. SVG/diagram title gradients are owned by the diagrams family (no-gradient-text-titles).
checks.impl_hint: Match rule blocks containing both /background(-image)?\s*:\s*[^;]*gradient/ and /-?(webkit-)?background-clip\s*:\s*text/

checks.title: Glow/pulse keyframes and infinite motion on static content
checks.applies_when: CSS contains `@keyframes` or an `animation`/`animation-iteration-count` declaration.
checks.spec: Fail if either: (a) a `@keyframes NAME{}` whose NAME matches /glow|pulse|breathe|shimmer/i AND whose body animates `box-shadow` or oscillates `opacity`/`filter`; or (b) any `animation:` shorthand / `animation-iteration-count: infinite` applied to a selector that is NOT a progress/loading/spinner element.
checks.fixture_violation: `@keyframes glow{0%{box-shadow:0 0 4px}50%{box-shadow:0 0 24px}}` applied via `animation:glow 2s infinite` to a `.card`.
checks.fp_guards: Exempt `.progress`/`.loading`/`.spinner`/[role=progressbar]; require infinite iteration or a glow/pulse-named box-shadow/opacity keyframe — do not flag one-shot user-triggered transitions. Mono-Industrial zero-onload-motion is owned by the mono family.
checks.impl_hint: Regex @keyframes bodies for box-shadow/opacity; regex /animation[^;]*infinite/ and check selector against progress allowlist.

checks.title: Emoji in section headers, status indicators, or table cells
checks.applies_when: Document has any heading, `.status*` indicator, or `<td>`.
checks.spec: Query own text content of section headings (h1–h6, .section-label, .section-header, .kicker), status indicators (.status, .status--*, .status-dot*), and table cells (`td`). Fail if any contains an emoji codepoint (U+1F300–1FAFF, U+2600–27BF, U+2B00–2BFF, U+1F000–1F0FF, U+FE0F, and ✅❌⚠️). Icons must be inline SVG or CSS shapes, not emoji glyphs.
checks.fixture_violation:
`

🚀 Architecture

` (or a `✅` status cell).
checks.fp_guards: Inspect each element's own text nodes only; styled <span>/inline-SVG status dots with no glyph pass; emoji inside <code>/<pre> shown as literal content is exempt.
checks.impl_hint: Emoji Unicode-range regex over el.textContent for the three selector groups.

checks.title: macOS traffic-light dots on code blocks
checks.applies_when: Page has a code-block header (`.code-block` header / `.code-file__head` / header preceding a `<pre>`) containing >=3 round sibling elements.
checks.spec: In a code-block header, detect exactly 3 small circular sibling elements (border-radius:50% or ~equal width/height <=16px) whose declared background-colours approximate the traffic-light triple (red ~#ff5f56, yellow ~#ffbd2e, green ~#27c93f, within tolerance). A red/yellow/green triple atop a code block = fail; use a filename/language label header instead.
checks.fixture_violation: Code-block header with three 12px spans coloured #ff5f56 / #ffbd2e / #27c93f.
checks.fp_guards: Must sit specifically on a code-block header, not a status legend; require the red/yellow/green colour signature, not any three dots.

checks.title: Unresolved template placeholders / generic stand-ins
checks.applies_when: Always.
checks.spec: Regex the final HTML for `{{[A-Za-z0-9_]+}}` and `{{NN}}` tokens, standalone `TODO`/`FIXME`, `Lorem ipsum`, and generic placeholder names `\bModule [A-Z]\b` / `\bComponent [A-Z]\b`. Any match outside a <code>/<pre> block that is demonstrably documenting template syntax = fail.
checks.fixture_violation: Body contains `{{skill_dir}}` or an h2 literally titled 'Module A'.
checks.fp_guards: Allow matches inside <code>/<pre> demonstrating template syntax; require the token to read as an unresolved placeholder (inside {{ }} or a Module/Component [A-Z] card title), not legitimate prose that mentions the word TODO.

checks.title: No external local asset references
checks.applies_when: Always (document has src=/href= attributes).
checks.spec: Scan all `src=`/`href=` attribute values; fail if any resolves to a relative/local filesystem path (does not start with http://, https://, data:, blob:, #, mailto:, tel:). CDN links (https) and data:/blob: URIs are allowed; the artifact must be one portable file.
checks.fixture_violation: `` or ``.
checks.fp_guards: Exclude in-page anchors (#…), mailto:, tel:; allow https/data/blob.

checks.title: Doctype / charset / viewport / title present
checks.applies_when: Artifact is a standalone HTML file (page/slides/magazine).
checks.spec: Per SKILL.md File Structure, assert the raw file starts with `<!DOCTYPE html>` (case-insensitive) and the parsed DOM contains `<meta charset>`, `<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">`, and a non-empty `<title>`. Any missing piece = fail.
checks.fixture_violation: Page missing the `<meta name="viewport">` tag (or an empty/absent `<title>`).
checks.fp_guards: None significant; poster (TSX→PNG) and video-comp compositions are excluded — they have their own root/canvas contracts.

checks.title: Both light and dark palettes defined
checks.applies_when: `:root` defines color-like custom properties (--bg/--surface/--text/--border/--accent/etc.).
checks.spec: Parse <style>. Collect color custom properties in the top-level `:root{}`. Require a dark override block — `@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark){:root{…}}` OR `:root[data-theme="dark"]{…}` — that redefines at least the core semantic set present in light (bg/surface/text/border/accent, case-insensitive substring). Fail if color custom props exist but no dark override block, or the dark block omits the core set.
checks.fixture_violation: `:root{--bg:#fff;--text:#111;--accent:#c33}` with no dark-mode block anywhere in the file.
checks.fp_guards: Skip pure-grayscale utility pages with zero color custom props; poster is single-theme by design and excluded.

checks.title: Links must not use UA-default color
checks.applies_when: Document has >=1 `<a href>` (excluding #, mailto:, tel:).
checks.spec: If the document contains real links, assert the CSS has an `a`/`a:link`/`.prose a` rule setting `color` to a non-initial value (ideally `var(--accent)`). Fail if links exist but no link-color override rule is present, since UA-default link blue looks unstyled and has poor contrast on dark backgrounds.
checks.fixture_violation: Page with body links and no `a{color:…}` rule (renders default blue).
checks.fp_guards: Skip entirely when the document has zero <a> tags; any color rule targeting links passes.

checks.title: Page-level .node class collides with Mermaid
checks.applies_when: Page uses Mermaid (`class="mermaid"` / `.mermaid` / `mermaid.initialize`).
checks.spec: Scan CSS selectors for a bare `.node` class token not nested under `.mermaid`, and scan HTML for an element whose class list contains the exact token `node`. Either = fail — Mermaid uses `.node` internally on SVG <g> elements, so page-level `.node` rules leak into diagrams; use `.ve-card` instead.
checks.fixture_violation: A Mermaid page with a page-level `.node:hover{transform:translateY(-2px)}` rule.
checks.fp_guards: Exact token boundary only — do not match `.node-list`/`.node-a`; `.mermaid .node` scoped rules are allowed; only enforce when Mermaid is present on the page.
checks.impl_hint: /(^|[\s,{])\.node(?![-\w])/ (excluding /^\.mermaid\s+\.node/) and class="([^"]*\s)?node(\s[^"]*)?"

checks.title: Code/tree blocks preserve whitespace
checks.applies_when: Page has a `<pre>`, `.code-block`, `.code-file__body`, or `.dir-tree`.
checks.spec: For CSS rules targeting block code containers (`pre`, `pre code`, `.code-block`, `.code-file__body`, `.dir-tree`), assert `white-space` is pre / pre-wrap / pre-line (never normal). Directory-tree / box-drawing containers (`.dir-tree`, or text containing ├└│─) must specifically be `pre` — pre-wrap soft-wraps and breaks connector alignment. Missing declaration or `normal` = fail.
checks.fixture_violation: A `.code-block` with no white-space declaration (defaults to normal, code runs together).
checks.fp_guards: Inline `<code>` inside running prose is exempt (normal is correct); scope to block-level code containers only.

checks.title: Long code needs collapse or max-height
checks.applies_when: Page has a `<pre><code>` block over ~100 lines or that would render taller than ~500px.
checks.spec: For `<pre><code>` blocks NOT inside `<details>`: (a) if newline-delimited line count > 100 → warn (avoid dumping full files; use outlines/collapsible/snippets); (b) if the block would render taller than ~500px, require `max-height` set and `overflow-y: auto|scroll`. Either unmet = warn.
checks.fixture_violation: A 150-line `<pre><code>` not wrapped in <details> and with no max-height/overflow.
checks.fp_guards: Exempt code inside `<details>`; short snippets exempt; slides/magazine paginate and are out of scope (scroll rule).

checks.title: Tabular data must use a real <table>
checks.applies_when: Mechanical prefilter matches: a div-based structure with >=3 sibling row-like divs each holding >=3 cell-like children with consistent per-column classes (>=4 rows or >=3 cols).
checks.spec: For each prefiltered div-grid region, feed the LLM that region's HTML plus a screenshot crop. Rubric question: 'Does this div-grid present row/column tabular data (comparison / audit / status matrix) that should be a semantic <table>, rather than a card grid / KPI row / feature grid?' A YES = warn (rebuild as <table>/<thead>/<tbody>).
checks.fixture_violation: A 4-row x 3-col comparison built from `<div class=row><div class=cell>` instead of a `<table>`.
checks.fp_guards: Card grids, KPI rows, and feature grids with varying per-card content are legitimately not tables and must pass — the LLM makes this call, not the prefilter.

checks.title: Prose font-size / line-height / measure floors
checks.applies_when: Page has a `.prose` reading-first region.
checks.spec:
Extend the 16px font-size / ≥1.5 line-height / ≤~75–85ch measure floors from `.prose p` to ALL running body prose (any multi-line

/list item that is not a caption/label/eyebrow/mono/code/table-cell), on page AND magazine profiles. Keep captions/labels/metadata/axis-labels exempt. Slides remain covered by min-slide-body-text-16px; poster/video-comp fixed canvases stay exempt (fixed-size type is intentional there).
checks.fixture_violation: `.prose p{font-size:13px;line-height:1.2}` in a prose article.
checks.fp_guards: Only `.prose` body copy; headings, labels, captions, and code are exempt; measure uses a tolerance band to avoid nagging borderline cases.
checks.spec_modification_note: The typeset tells (ai-sub-16px-body-text, ai-unbounded-line-length, ai-default-tight-line-height) are ~80% covered by prose-readability-minimums but only inside a .prose container. Widening the selector to all running prose (still excluding secondary roles) folds those three tells in without a new check, and the profile guard keeps fixed-canvas posters/videos out.

checks.title: Pull-quote / drop-cap restraint
checks.applies_when: Page uses a pull-quote (`.pullquote`/`.pull-quote`/`blockquote.pull`) or drop-cap (`.lead--dropcap`/`::first-letter`) pattern.
checks.spec: Count pull-quote elements per rendered page — warn if > 2. Count drop-cap usages — warn if applied to more than the single lead paragraph (> 1 section). Either exceeded = warn.
checks.fixture_violation: A page with 3 `.pullquote` elements.
checks.fp_guards: One drop cap on the article lead is correct; <=2 pull quotes is fine; scope the pull-quote count per rendered page/slide, not across a whole multi-slide deck.

checks.title: No console errors or failed asset loads on load
checks.applies_when: Always (renderable HTML artifact).
checks.spec: Load the page headlessly in both light and dark schemes; collect console messages of type 'error' plus failed network requests (non-2xx on linked resources). Fail on any error-level console message or non-2xx asset load, except a documented allowlist. Retry transient CDN 404s once before failing.
checks.fixture_violation: Page with a top-level `throw new Error()` on load, or a script that logs a Mermaid parse error.
checks.fp_guards: Retry once for CDN flakiness (Google Fonts/jsDelivr rate limits); allow documented expected warnings; only 'error' level counts.

checks.title: Visual hierarchy survives the squint test
checks.applies_when: Always (page/slides/magazine renders).
checks.spec: Capture a full-page screenshot at 1440x900 (light), programmatically blur/downscale it. Rubric question (fed only the blurred image plus the page's declared content type): 'In this blurred view, is there a clear dominant focal region with quieter subordinate regions, or does the page read as uniformly flat with no visual hierarchy?' FLAT = warn.
checks.fixture_violation: A page where every section uses identical type size, weight, and spacing (deliberately flat).
checks.fp_guards: Dense reference / audit / data-table pages may legitimately be flatter — judge against the page's own stated content type, not a hero bar. Mono-specific three-layer rules are owned by the mono family.

checks.title: AI-slop telltale phrases in prose
checks.applies_when: Page has prose text nodes (paragraphs / leads / callouts / card descriptions / slide body copy).
checks.spec: Replace the small inline literal list with the curated high-precision phrase set now owned by the new F9 check `copy-slop-phrases` (see phrase_check). Keep unslop-prose-phrases responsible ONLY for the frequency heuristic it uniquely does — flag high-frequency 'however'/'moreover' (>1 per 300 words) — and delegate all fixed-phrase literals to copy-slop-phrases so the two checks do not double-fire on the same text. Same excluded-prose filter (no code/labels/table-headers/Mermaid/[BRACKET]).
checks.fixture_violation: A body paragraph containing 'It's important to note that...'.
checks.fp_guards: Exclude Space Mono ALL-CAPS labels, table headers, code, Mermaid node labels, and bracketed system messages so terse technical labels don't trigger.
checks.spec_modification_note: The tell corpus expands the phrase list far beyond v1's six literals. Rather than grow two overlapping literal lists, consolidate all fixed phrases in copy-slop-phrases and let unslop-prose-phrases retain only its distinct frequency check, preventing duplicate warns on one paragraph.

checks.title: AI-slop stylistic rhythm beyond fixed phrases
checks.applies_when: Page has prose (same excluded-filter set as unslop-prose-phrases).
checks.spec: Concatenate the excluded-filtered prose text as the artifact slice. Rubric question: 'Beyond fixed phrases, does this prose show AI-slop stylistic tells — manufactured emphasis, predictable three-item list rhythm, hollow however/moreover transitions, empty summary sentences — that /unslop would rewrite?' YES = warn.
checks.fixture_violation: Prose with a forced three-item list plus hollow transition rhythm that no fixed phrase catches.
checks.fp_guards: Terse technical labels/captions excluded; judge only sustained prose, not one-line UI copy.

checks.title: prefers-reduced-motion block when animating
checks.applies_when: The <style> contains a `@keyframes` or an `animation:`/`transition:` with a non-zero duration.
checks.spec: If the stylesheet defines any animation/transition with duration > 0, assert it also contains an `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce){…}` block that collapses animation/transition durations to near-zero for the animated elements or `*`. Missing = warn.
checks.fixture_violation: A page with a fade-in `@keyframes` / `transition:all .4s` and no prefers-reduced-motion block.
checks.fp_guards: Static non-animated pages have nothing to guard and are exempt; video-comp is excluded (GSAP/Hyperframes drive motion externally).

checks.title: Page-level overflow-x clip contract on html/body
checks.applies_when: Page profile only; skip when the artifact is fixed-canvas (root/body has fixed px width+height matching a slide/poster/magazine-deck canvas, a data-layout=slide|magazine|poster marker, or the document declares no @media(max-width) query at all).
checks.spec: In the concatenated <style> text, locate the rule(s) targeting html and body (combined `html,body` or separate). Require ALL: `overflow-x:hidden` AND `overflow-x:clip` both declared, with the clip declaration appearing AFTER hidden in source order (progressive enhancement); `max-width:100%`; `margin:0`; `padding:0` covering html/body. Additionally assert NO rule targeting html|body|:root sets `overflow-x:scroll` (regex `(?:html|body|:root)[^{]*\{[^}]*overflow-x:\s*scroll` anywhere ⇒ violation). Browser corroboration (when a DOM is available): getComputedStyle(document.body).overflowX and getComputedStyle(document.documentElement).overflowX must each resolve to 'clip' or 'hidden', never 'scroll' or 'visible'. Fail if any required clause is missing or the scroll negative matches.
checks.fixture_violation: Page whose `html,body` block declares only `overflow-x:hidden` (no following `overflow-x:clip`); a second fixture sets `overflow-x:scroll` on body.
checks.impl_hint: Match html/body block, then assert index of /overflow-x:\s*clip/ > index of /overflow-x:\s*hidden/; separate negative regex for scroll on html|body|:root.
checks.fp_guards: Accept clip+hidden split across separate html{} and body{} rules; accept a covering universal rule for margin/padding. Do not require the exact whitespace of the doc's code block.

checks.title: Universal box-sizing:border-box reset present
checks.applies_when: Page profile, non-fixed-canvas (same profile guard as page-overflow-clip-contract).
checks.spec: Regex the concatenated <style> for a universal border-box reset: a selector group covering `*` (optionally with `*::before,*::after`) whose declaration block contains `box-sizing:\s*border-box`. Also accept the inherit idiom (`html{box-sizing:border-box}` plus `*,*::before,*::after{box-sizing:inherit}`) or an equivalent reset-library rule. Fail only if no universal border-box coverage exists anywhere.
checks.fixture_violation: Page whose <style> contains no box-sizing declaration on `*` (or any universal selector).
checks.fp_guards: Do not hard-require the exact three-selector string; any rule achieving universal border-box coverage counts.

checks.title: Body wraps long tokens (overflow-wrap/word-break)
checks.applies_when: Page profile, non-fixed-canvas.
checks.spec: Regex for a `body` rule containing `overflow-wrap:\s*(anywhere|break-word)` or `word-break:\s*break-word`. Warn if absent. Advisory only — the authoritative failure for long-token overflow is the measured body-overflow gate; equivalent per-element `word-break` on long-token elements also satisfies intent.
checks.fixture_violation: Page with a `body{...}` rule that sets no overflow-wrap/word-break and no per-element wrapping.
checks.fp_guards: Downgraded to warn (not error) because the real ship-blocker is measured overflow; targeted word-break:break-all on the specific long-token element is an acceptable alternative.

checks.title: Grid tracks beside fixed-px children use minmax(0,1fr) not bare 1fr
checks.applies_when: Page profile; only when a grid-template-columns declaration pairs a bare 1fr with a fixed-px track.
checks.spec: Scan <style> grid-template-columns declarations for a bare `1fr` track directly adjacent to a fixed-px track and NOT wrapped in minmax(): match `grid-template-columns:\s*[^;]*\b\d+px\s+1fr\b` or `\b1fr\s+\d+px\b` within a declaration where that `1fr` is not the tail of a `minmax(` call. Any match is a violation (documented #1 root cause of horizontal overflow).
checks.fixture_violation: A non-mobile rule with `grid-template-columns: 260px 1fr;`.
checks.fp_guards: Ignore `1fr` inside `minmax(0, 1fr)` and inside `repeat(auto-fit, minmax(...))`; only flag when a fixed-px track shares the same declaration.

checks.title: Mobile breakpoints limited to sanctioned values
checks.applies_when: Page profile with at least one `@media (max-width: Npx)` query.
checks.spec: Extract every `@media` `max-width:\s*(\d+)px` value. Allowed standard set {768, 820}; sanctioned exceptions {640 (Nothing grid-lines), 1000 (responsive-nav.md TOC sidebar→bar collapse)}. Warn on any other max-width value.
checks.fixture_violation: Page whose only breakpoint is `@media (max-width: 900px)`.
checks.fp_guards: Whitelist the sanctioned aesthetic/nav exceptions; do not flag min-width companion queries or non-width media features.

checks.title: .pipeline keeps flex-wrap:nowrap at mobile breakpoint
checks.applies_when: Page contains an element with class `pipeline`.
checks.spec: Inside a mobile `@media (max-width:...)` block, the `.pipeline` rule must declare `flex-wrap:\s*nowrap`. Warn if .pipeline exists on the page but the mobile rule is missing or sets flex-wrap:wrap.
checks.fixture_violation: Page with a `.pipeline` element and a mobile media block that sets `.pipeline{flex-wrap:wrap}`.

checks.title: .kpi-row uses auto-fit minmax floor at mobile
checks.applies_when: Page contains an element with class `kpi-row`.
checks.spec: Inside the mobile media query, `.kpi-row` should set `grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(<N>px, 1fr))` (N tolerant). Warn if .kpi-row is present but reduces to a fixed column count at mobile instead of an auto-fit minmax floor.
checks.fixture_violation: `.kpi-row` with `grid-template-columns: repeat(4,1fr)` and no auto-fit override at mobile.
checks.fp_guards: Exact 140px floor is not required; any auto-fit minmax floor satisfies intent (that is why this is warn).

checks.title: Body never scrolls horizontally (390 + 1440, both schemes)
checks.applies_when: Page profile only; skip fixed-canvas artifacts (slide/magazine deck, poster, video-comp) detected via fixed canvas size / data-layout marker / absence of any @media(max-width).
checks.spec: Load the page and, at BOTH viewports 390x844 and 1440x900, in BOTH prefers-color-scheme light and dark, assert `document.documentElement.scrollWidth - window.innerWidth <= 1` AND `document.body.scrollWidth - window.innerWidth <= 1`. Fail if the 1px tolerance is exceeded at any viewport/scheme combination. (1px tolerance replaces the doc's strict === to absorb browser sub-pixel rounding.)
checks.fixture_violation: Page containing a 900px-wide fixed-width element (or wide <table>) with no scroll-x wrapper, so body scrollWidth exceeds 390 at mobile.
checks.impl_hint: page.evaluate(()=>({doc:document.documentElement.scrollWidth,view:window.innerWidth,body:document.body.scrollWidth}))

checks.title: No visibly-overflowing element (attribute the overflow source)
checks.applies_when: Page profile, non-fixed-canvas. Runs alongside the body gate to attribute the culprit.
checks.spec: At 390x844 (and 1440x900) in both schemes, evaluate `[...document.querySelectorAll('body *')].filter(el => el.clientWidth>0 && el.scrollWidth > el.clientWidth + 1 && getComputedStyle(el).overflowX === 'visible')`. The list must be empty; report {tag, className, scrollWidth, clientWidth} for each offender so the fix (add .scroll-x / min-width:0 / max-width:100%) can be targeted. Non-empty ⇒ fail.
checks.fixture_violation: An element wider than its parent styled `overflow-x:visible` with no scroll wrapper (e.g. a fixed-width inner div inside a narrower column).
checks.fp_guards: clientWidth>0 excludes display:none/zero-size nodes; overflowX==='visible' filter excludes intentional .scroll-x/auto scrollers.

checks.title: Grid/flex children can shrink (min-width:0) when they overflow
checks.applies_when: Page contains a container whose class matches /(^|\s)(grid|flex)(\s|$)/ or /-grid|-row/.
checks.spec: At 390x844, for each direct child of such a container, if child.scrollWidth > child.clientWidth + 1 (child is overflowing its own box) AND getComputedStyle(child).minWidth resolves to a non-zero value (i.e. min-width:auto), flag as a shrink-refusing child. Gate strictly on measured child overflow to avoid failing grids whose children are naturally narrow.
checks.fixture_violation: A `.grid` with `grid-template-columns:minmax(auto,1fr) 1fr` whose child holds a long unbroken token and computes min-width:auto, overflowing at 390.
checks.fp_guards: Do not flag on class presence alone; only when the child actually overflows and lacks min-width:0 (per family charter: cross-check measured overflow before hard-failing).

checks.title: Fixed multi-column grids collapse to one column at mobile
checks.applies_when: Page contains an element matching arch-grid|comparison|diff-panels|dir-compare or [class$="-grid"], OR a hero using the desktop `minmax(0,1fr) <px>` two-track pattern. Exclude .kpi-row.
checks.spec: At 390x844 (representing <=768px), for each such element assert getComputedStyle(el).gridTemplateColumns resolves to a SINGLE track (value is a single length/none, no internal space between track sizes). Covers both fixed-column named grids and the 2-column magazine hero. Skip .kpi-row (auto-fit exception).
checks.fixture_violation: `.arch-grid{grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr}` with no mobile override, still two tracks at 390.
checks.fp_guards: Exclude .kpi-row; only apply the hero clause when the desktop CSS actually shows the `minmax(0,1fr) <px>` two-track signature.

checks.title: Wide content scrolls inside its own container, not the page
checks.applies_when: Page contains a <table>, .pipeline, .dir-tree, an inline diagram <svg>, or a .code-block.
checks.spec: At 390x844, for each candidate wide element (table, .pipeline, .dir-tree pre, inline diagram svg, .code-block) that actually overflows (el.scrollWidth > el.clientWidth + 1), verify the element itself OR an ancestor within 3 levels has computed overflow-x in {auto, scroll} (i.e. a scroll container such as .scroll-x/.table-wrap/.mermaid-wrap or inline overflow-x:auto). If it overflows and no scroll container is found ⇒ violation. This subsumes the ~640px SVG threshold using measured overflow rather than the raw viewBox number.
checks.fixture_violation: A ~1000px-wide <table> placed directly in body with no .scroll-x / .table-wrap wrapper.
checks.fp_guards: Gate every flag on measured overflow, never on tag/class presence; content that fits at 390 needs no wrapper. An SVG with width:100%;height:auto that scales down is not flagged.

checks.title: Directory-tree connectors never wrap; they scroll
checks.applies_when: Page has a .dir-tree element, or a <pre> whose text contains box-drawing characters (├ └ │).
checks.spec: For each such <pre>, assert getComputedStyle(el).whiteSpace is 'pre' or 'nowrap' (NOT 'pre-wrap' or 'normal') AND the element or an ancestor within 3 levels has overflow-x auto/scroll. Violation if wrapping is permitted (pre-wrap/normal) OR no horizontal scroll container exists.
checks.fixture_violation: `.dir-tree pre{white-space:pre-wrap}` so the ├── connectors line-wrap and lose alignment.
checks.fp_guards: Scope to .dir-tree or box-drawing-char pres only; ordinary code <pre> may legitimately use pre-wrap.

checks.title: Detect text clipped by overflow clip/hidden (mechanical)
checks.applies_when: Page profile, non-fixed-canvas.
checks.spec: At 390x844 (both schemes) collect elements where getComputedStyle overflowX or overflowY is 'clip'/'hidden' AND (scrollWidth>clientWidth+1 OR scrollHeight>clientHeight+1) AND the element has non-empty direct text content, EXCLUDING elements whose computed `-webkit-line-clamp` is not 'none' or `text-overflow` is 'ellipsis'. Emit the candidate list for the paired llm-pass. Non-empty ⇒ warn (visual confirmation decides the ship-blocker).
checks.fixture_violation: A heading inside a fixed-width box with overflow:hidden that chops characters, with no ellipsis/line-clamp.
checks.fp_guards: Exclude intentional -webkit-line-clamp and text-overflow:ellipsis truncation from candidates.

checks.title: LLM: real text is visibly cut off by a container
checks.applies_when: Runs when text-clip-candidates produced a non-empty list (consumes that list + 390px screenshots).
checks.spec: Rubric question, answerable from the 390x844 light+dark screenshots plus the text-clip candidate element list: 'Is any real content text visibly cut off by a container edge — characters chopped or a sentence truncated WITHOUT an intentional ellipsis?' Yes ⇒ fail. No page-wide context needed.
checks.fixture_violation: N/A (LLM pass) — exercised by feeding a screenshot where a heading is chopped mid-word by an overflow:hidden box.

checks.title: Detect content overlapped by fixed-position chrome (mechanical)
checks.applies_when: Page contains at least one position:fixed element (e.g. the theme toggle).
checks.spec: At 390x844 (both schemes), for each position:fixed element with a non-transparent computed background, compute its bounding rect and find interactive/text elements (a, button, h1-h4, p, td, .sec-head) whose rects geometrically intersect it and paint beneath it. Emit overlap candidates for the paired llm-pass. Non-empty ⇒ warn.
checks.fixture_violation: A fixed theme toggle docked top-right whose rect overlaps an <h1> at 390 width.
checks.fp_guards: Require non-transparent background on the fixed element; overlap with pure padding/empty area is not a candidate.

checks.title: LLM: fixed chrome actually obscures readable/clickable content
checks.applies_when: Runs when content-behind-fixed-ui-candidates is non-empty (consumes overlaps + 390px screenshots).
checks.spec: Rubric question from the 390x844 screenshots + overlap candidate list: 'Does fixed-position chrome (theme toggle, nav, counter) actually hide readable text or block a clickable target at mobile width?' Yes ⇒ fail.
checks.fixture_violation: N/A (LLM pass) — exercised by a screenshot where the theme toggle sits on top of the page title.

checks.title: .scroll-x not misapplied to prose / hero text
checks.applies_when: Page profile, non-fixed-canvas.
checks.spec:
Assert no

element and no hero text-bearing element carries class `scroll-x`; assert card-grid containers are not themselves wrapped in `.scroll-x` (they should reflow to one column). A `.scroll-x` wrapper around a wide diagram that happens to live inside the hero is allowed — scope to text-bearing prose nodes, not any .hero descendant.
checks.fixture_violation:
`

` on a prose paragraph.

checks.title: TOC nav structural + min-width:0 contract
checks.applies_when: Page has both a `.wrap` element and a `nav.toc`.
checks.spec: Assert .wrap.firstElementChild is nav.toc (tag nav, classList includes 'toc'); assert all section content lives inside a `.main` child of .wrap (every .sec-head is a descendant of .main, none are direct children of .wrap); and assert the stylesheet contains a `.main{...min-width:0...}` declaration. Fail if the toc is not first, content leaks outside .main, or .main lacks min-width:0.
checks.fixture_violation: `.wrap` whose first child is `.main` with the `nav.toc` second, or a `.main` rule missing `min-width:0`.
checks.fp_guards: Out of scope for pages that do not use the .wrap+.toc+.main nav pattern.

checks.title: TOC links resolve 1:1 to section ids
checks.applies_when: Page has a `nav.toc`.
checks.spec: Assert every `.sec-head` element has a non-empty id; every `.toc a[href^="#"]` (excluding a '#'/'#top' back-to-top link) resolves via document.getElementById to an existing element; and the count of section .toc links equals the count of .sec-head elements.
checks.fixture_violation: A TOC link `href="#s3"` with no element carrying `id="s3"`.
checks.fp_guards: Exclude utility links (back-to-top) from the 1:1 count.

checks.title: TOC omitted for pages with <4 sections
checks.applies_when: Page has a `nav.toc`.
checks.spec: Count .sec-head elements (or section .toc links). Warn if fewer than 4 — a near-empty TOC adds clutter.
checks.fixture_violation: Page with a `.toc` and only 2 `.sec-head` sections.
checks.fp_guards: Warn (not error): a page with few top-level sections but many subsections may still justify a TOC.

checks.title: pretext label measurement waits for fonts.ready
checks.applies_when: Source contains a `prepareWithSegments(` call (pretext label-measurement library in use).
checks.spec: For each `prepareWithSegments(` call, require an awaited `document.fonts.ready` or `document.fonts.load(` earlier in the same script scope (before the call). Warn on any prepareWithSegments with no preceding fonts-ready await — measuring against fallback fonts yields wrong widths that drift every downstream box calc.
checks.fixture_violation: A script calling `prepareWithSegments(...)` with no `await document.fonts.ready`/`document.fonts.load` beforehand.
checks.fp_guards: Not applicable to the vast majority of pages that never reference pretext/prepareWithSegments.

checks.title: SVG diagram fills/strokes use semantic tokens, not hardcoded hex
checks.applies_when: Document contains an inline <svg> authored by the skill (heuristic: an <svg> whose element attributes reference var(--...) tokens, or that declares data-diagram-role).
checks.spec: Regex diagram-body elements for hex color literals (#[0-9a-fA-F]{3,8}) and rgb()/rgba() literals appearing inside fill=, stroke=, or style= attributes. Exclude the :root/@media token-definition blocks and url(#...) references. Flag any literal color on a body element.
checks.impl_hint: /(?:fill|stroke)\s*=\s*"(#[0-9a-fA-F]{3,8}|rgba?\()/ and same inside style="..."; skip nodes inside <style>/:root.
checks.fp_guards: Allowlist the Nothing dot-grid radial-gradient color when those aesthetics are declared; ignore url(#id) marker/gradient references.
checks.fixture_violation: A diagram <rect fill="#b5523a"> (accent hardcoded) instead of fill="var(--accent)", with no Nothing aesthetic declared.

checks.title: No shadows, gradient-text, emoji, animated shadows, vertical arrow text, or 16px radius in diagrams
checks.applies_when: Document contains an inline skill-authored <svg> diagram.
checks.spec: Regex-scan diagram markup and flag any of: (a) box-shadow or SVG feDropShadow on nodes; (b) background-clip:text / -webkit-background-clip:text co-occurring with a linear-gradient on a title/heading; (c) pictographic emoji codepoints inside <text> content; (d) box-shadow or filter:blur combined with animation/transition/@keyframes (animated shadow); (e) writing-mode:vertical on arrow-associated <text>; (f) Tailwind class rounded-2xl or an rx/ry/border-radius of exactly 16.
checks.fp_guards: Exempt the flowchart oval rx=20 and Nothing's documented dot-grid radial-gradient; the emoji scan targets pictographic ranges only (allow →, ↑, ↓, math arrows); static hairline strokes are fine.
checks.fixture_violation: A node <rect style="box-shadow:0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,.2)"> in a diagram.

checks.title: JetBrains Mono absent from diagrams (except declared IDE-inspired aesthetics)
checks.applies_when: Inline skill-authored <svg> diagram present AND the active aesthetic is NOT an IDE-inspired one whose canonical palette specifies JetBrains Mono.
checks.spec: Regex for "JetBrains Mono" in any font-family declaration (inline style, @font-face, or Google Fonts href) within diagram markup. Flag presence.
checks.fp_guards: Aesthetic-scoped exception (decision b): when the page declares an IDE-inspired aesthetic (Dracula/Nord/Catppuccin/Solarized/Gruvbox/One Dark/Rose Pine) whose published palette maps --font-mono to JetBrains Mono, the font is permitted.
checks.fixture_violation: A default Editorial-Diagram sets --font-mono:"JetBrains Mono" with no IDE-inspired aesthetic declared.

checks.title: Diagram geometry divisible by 4 (radii and strokes exempt)
checks.applies_when: Inline skill-authored <svg> diagram present.
checks.spec: Parse numeric values of x, y, cx, cy, width, height, font-size, and gap/spacing across diagram SVG; assert each value % 4 === 0. Report every offending element+attribute+value.
checks.impl_hint: Skip attributes: stroke-width, stroke-dasharray, all *-opacity, rx, ry; skip the 22x22 dot-pattern width/height and its r=0.9 dot.
checks.fp_guards: Per decision (a): corner radii rx/ry are EXEMPT from the 4px rule (they carry per-shape semantic values 6/8/20 validated by diagram-shape-semantics); stroke-width, dasharray, opacity, and the 22x22 / r=0.9 dot pattern are exempt.
checks.fixture_violation: <rect x="13" y="40" width="100" height="40"> where x=13 is not divisible by 4.

checks.title: Node names/titles use body sans, monospace reserved for technical labels
checks.applies_when: Inline diagram with node-name/title/description text identifiable (data-diagram-role tagging present) AND aesthetic is NOT Monochrome-Terminal. Warn-level heuristic fallback when tagging absent.
checks.spec: For every <text> classified as a node name/title/description, resolve font-family and assert it is var(--font-body), not var(--font-mono). Additionally flag if var(--font-mono) is the majority face across body-classified text. Technical labels (ports/types/URLs/arrow labels/eyebrow kickers) may be mono.
checks.fp_guards: Monochrome-Terminal aesthetic explicitly permits mono as body font — skip there. Requires role tagging to distinguish node names from technical labels; degrade to warn when absent.
checks.fixture_violation: A node title <text data-diagram-role="node-name" font-family="var(--font-mono)">Auth Service</text>.

checks.title: Embedded diagram inherits host tokens, does not redefine :root
checks.applies_when: A diagram <svg>/<style> lives inside a document that already declares a :root token block (--paper/--ink) earlier in the cascade.
checks.spec: Flag duplicate :root declarations of the diagram color-token names (--paper/--ink/etc.) within a single document — an embedded diagram must inherit host tokens rather than redeclaring them.
checks.fixture_violation: Page :root defines --paper/--ink; an embedded diagram's <style> redeclares :root{--paper:#fff;...}.

checks.title: Arrows painted before nodes so nodes sit on top
checks.applies_when: Arrows and nodes are distinguishable via data-diagram-role or separate <g> groups.
checks.spec: Assert the arrows group's document index precedes the nodes group; or, per connected component, assert every arrow-classified element's document index precedes the node elements it connects.
checks.fp_guards: Requires role tagging or a group convention to classify; fall back to group-level ordering assertion when only <g> grouping exists.
checks.fixture_violation: The <g data-diagram-role="nodes"> element appears before <g data-diagram-role="arrows"> in document order.

checks.title: Legend is a bottom horizontal strip below a hairline, with viewBox headroom
checks.applies_when: A legend group is present in the diagram.
checks.spec: Assert the legend group bbox sits within the bottom ~60px band of the viewBox; items lay out left-to-right (width >> height, x-coords monotonic and non-overlapping); a sibling line/rect with stroke=var(--rule) sits directly above it; and viewBox height >= bodyMaxY + ~50-70px headroom. Never float inside the diagram body.
checks.fp_guards: The ~60px headroom is approximate — allow a 50-70px band before flagging headroom.
checks.fixture_violation: A legend group positioned mid-canvas (y in the middle of the viewBox) as a vertical stack.

checks.title: Each arrow label preceded by an opaque paper mask rect
checks.applies_when: Arrow-label text is present (role tagging or arrow-associated <text>).
checks.spec: For every <text> classified as an arrow label, assert the immediately-preceding sibling is a <rect> with fill resolving to var(--paper)/var(--paper-2). (Pixel-coverage of the rect over the text bbox is verified in diagram-text-clipping-overlap.)
checks.fixture_violation: An arrow label <text data-diagram-role="arrow-label">retry</text> with no preceding mask <rect>.

checks.title: Diagram within universal density budget (<=9 nodes, <=12 arrows)
checks.applies_when: Elements carry data-diagram-role tagging (decision c). Warn-level heuristic count fallback when tagging absent.
checks.spec: Count generic node elements (role=node), assert <=9; count arrow/transition elements (marker-end=url(#arrow*) or role=arrow), assert <=12. Exclude legend/annotation/axis elements from the node count.
checks.fp_guards: Reliable only with role tagging; without it, degrade to a warn heuristic (count all <rect> minus legend/background). Nodes/arrows in a Mermaid-rendered SVG count too (decision e).
checks.fixture_violation: A diagram with 11 <rect data-diagram-role="node"> elements.

checks.title: Accent color on at most 2 elements
checks.applies_when: Inline diagram where the accent token/literal is resolvable.
checks.spec: Count distinct elements whose fill or stroke resolves to var(--accent) (or the aesthetic's literal accent hex); assert count <= 2.
checks.fp_guards: do not double-count the arrow-accent marker <defs> definition. Applies equally to Mermaid-rendered SVG (decision e).
checks.fixture_violation: Three separate <rect> elements each with fill="var(--accent)".

checks.title: Per-type element-count budgets
checks.applies_when: Diagram type is detectable (data-diagram-type or structural signature) AND role tagging present. Warn-level fallback when tagging absent.
checks.spec: Per detected type, assert count <= budget: sequence lifelines <=5; swimlane lanes <=5; ER entities <=8; nested containment levels <=6; tree depth <=4 and children-per-node <=5; layer-stack layers 4-6; venn set-circles <=3; pyramid/funnel layers 4-6; annotation callouts <=2; quadrant items <=12.
checks.fp_guards: Quadrant (~12) and nested-level counts are hedged in the docs — apply +1 tolerance before flagging. Venn circle count excludes dot/annotation markers. This family is the canonical owner of these budgets; skill-md duplicates (venn-max-3, swimlane-max-5, quadrant, tree, pyramid) defer here (decision d).
checks.fixture_violation: A venn diagram with 4 set-circles (data-diagram-type="venn").

checks.title: Flowchart/state/sequence shapes carry their conventional geometry
checks.applies_when: Detected diagram type is flowchart, state-machine, or sequence.
checks.spec: Flowchart: start/end rx=20, step rx=6, decision=diamond with <=3 outgoing arrows (else must nest), merge=filled <circle r=4>; every decision outgoing arrow has a text label; shape (not color) must be the semantic channel. State-machine: state rects rx=8, start=filled <circle r=6>, end=two concentric circles, transition label matches /^\S+(\s\[[^\]]+\])?\s*/\s*\S+/, transitions <= 2x state count, no 'from any state' fan-out. Sequence: lifelines vertical (x1~x2) with stroke-dasharray set, message arrows horizontal (y1~y2) with non-decreasing y in document order, activation bars width=8 stroke-width=0.8 fill~var(--muted) each with matched open+close, return arrows dashed, no lane dividers.
checks.fp_guards: The per-shape rx values (6/8/20) are the authoritative spec and are exempt from the 4px grid (decision a). Requires type detection + role tagging.
checks.fixture_violation: A flowchart step <rect data-diagram-role="step" rx="12"> (rx should be 6).

checks.title: Quadrant plot: centered cross, end labels, dots off-axis, uniform quadrant fill
checks.applies_when: Detected diagram type is quadrant.
checks.spec: Assert axis lines cross at the plot-area geometric center; axis labels lie near the extreme x/y of each axis (not within a midpoint tolerance band); item dots have r=4; no item (x,y) sits within epsilon of x=centerX or y=centerY; any quadrant background rects all share the same fill.
checks.fixture_violation: A quadrant item dot placed exactly on the vertical axis line, or the four quadrants filled with four different colors.

checks.title: Annotation callouts: italic serif, dashed leader, landing dot, <=2, margins only
checks.applies_when: An annotation-callout structure is present.
checks.spec: Callout text font-style=italic and font-family=var(--font-display); leader path has stroke-dasharray set (never solid); a landing-dot <circle> exists at the leader terminus; count <=2 per diagram; neutral callout leader stroke-opacity~0.4 and text fill=var(--ink), focal callout leader+text fill=var(--accent). Non-overlap with content is verified in diagram-text-clipping-overlap.
checks.fixture_violation: An annotation callout with a solid (non-dashed) leader line.

checks.title: Sketchy displacement filter applies to shapes only, never text
checks.applies_when: A <filter> using feTurbulence/feDisplacementMap is defined.
checks.spec: Assert filter params baseFrequency in [0.01,0.04], numOctaves in [1,3], scale in [1,6], and a fixed seed attribute present. Find every element referencing that filter (directly or via a filtered ancestor); assert no <text> is among them or their descendants.
checks.fixture_violation: A <text filter="url(#sketch)"> or a filtered <g> that contains <text>.

checks.title: Proportional diagram geometry matches its data
checks.applies_when: A ranked/proportional diagram (pyramid, funnel, venn, timeline, bar) whose items carry data-value / data-percentage / data-cardinality attributes.
checks.spec: Recompute expected geometry from the data and assert rendered geometry matches within a few px: pyramid/funnel layer width = maxWidth x (value/base); venn circle radius proportional to sqrt(cardinality); timeline event x-position proportional to real time delta across adjacent events (or a visible axis-break marker over anomalous gaps). Accent must not sit on the base/largest layer; widths monotonic in a single direction.
checks.fp_guards: Runs only when machine-readable data-* attributes exist; otherwise defer to diagram-proportional-honesty-visual (llm).
checks.fixture_violation: A funnel layer with data-percentage="72" rendered at full canvas width instead of 0.72 x maxWidth.

checks.title: Arrow tips stop 6-10px from node borders, never inside
checks.applies_when: Arrows are tagged/detectable and connect to nodes with resolvable bounding boxes.
checks.spec: Render at 1440x900 (both schemes). For each arrow terminal point and its connected node getBBox(), compute perpendicular distance from the endpoint to the nearest node edge and assert 6-10px; assert the endpoint fails a point-in-rect test against its own node bbox and every neighboring node bbox.
checks.fp_guards: Requires role tagging to pair arrows with nodes.
checks.fixture_violation: An arrow whose arrowhead point lands 0px inside (piercing) the target node's border.

checks.title: No clipped or overlapping diagram text; mask rects cover labels
checks.applies_when: Inline skill-authored <svg> diagram present.
checks.spec: Render (1440x900, both schemes). For every <text> assert getBBox() is fully contained within the SVG viewBox and its intended container group's bbox. Pairwise collision-test all text bboxes and leader-line paths and assert no unexpected intersections (excluding intentional label-on-mask overlaps). For each arrow-label mask rect assert it contains its text bbox with >= ~4px margin per side.
checks.fp_guards: Exclude the intentional arrow-label text-over-mask overlap from the collision test.
checks.fixture_violation: A node label whose text extends past the node rect and past the viewBox edge, or two labels whose bboxes overlap.

checks.title: Legend entries match what the figure actually draws
checks.applies_when: A legend is present in the diagram.
checks.spec: Rubric question over the per-figure screenshot: 'Does every legend entry (swatch + label) correspond to something actually drawn in the figure, and does every distinct visual category in the figure appear in the legend? Flag any ghost legend item (in legend, not in figure) or missing item (in figure, not in legend).' Evidence: the per-figure screenshot only.
checks.fixture_violation: A legend lists a 'Cache' swatch color that appears nowhere in the drawn figure.

checks.title: Exactly one clearly dominant focal element
checks.applies_when: Inline skill-authored <svg> diagram present.
checks.spec: Rubric question over the per-figure screenshot: 'Is there exactly ONE clearly dominant focal element (the look-here-first point)? Flag if there is no clear focal point, or if two-or-more elements compete equally for primary attention.' Pairs with the deterministic diagram-focal-accent-count. Evidence: the per-figure screenshot only.
checks.fixture_violation: A diagram where two equally-weighted accent nodes compete with no single dominant focal.

checks.title: Visual proportions match stated quantities (no fabricated sizes)
checks.applies_when: A ranked/proportional diagram (pyramid, funnel, venn, timeline, bar) that lacks machine-readable data-* attributes.
checks.spec: Rubric question over the per-figure screenshot plus extracted label text: 'Do the relative sizes and spacings visually match the quantities stated in the labels (percentages, counts, dates)? Flag fabricated-equal layers, mis-scaled circles, or a timeline implying uniform pacing when the labels are not evenly spaced.' Evidence: per-figure screenshot + extracted label text.
checks.fixture_violation: A funnel whose three layers are drawn equal width but labeled 80% / 45% / 12%.

checks.title: Figure reads as a single coherent diagram type
checks.applies_when: Inline skill-authored <svg> or Mermaid diagram present.
checks.spec: Rubric question over the per-figure screenshot + brief: 'Does this figure read as a single coherent diagram type matching its content shape, rather than a muddle of two types? And if Mermaid was used instead of hand-authored SVG, was it warranted (15+ node graph, uniform-shape forest, or explicit user request)?' Evidence: per-figure screenshot + one-line content brief.
checks.fixture_violation: A figure that mixes swimlane lanes with a nested-containment metaphor in one canvas.

checks.title: Every element carries information; no padded or all-identical boxes
checks.applies_when: Inline skill-authored <svg> diagram present.
checks.spec: Rubric question over the per-figure screenshot: 'Is every element carrying information — could any node, label, or line be removed with no loss of meaning? Flag over-complex or padded diagrams, three equal-width summary cards, and all-identical boxes that erase visual hierarchy.' Evidence: the per-figure screenshot only.
checks.fixture_violation: A diagram of five identically-sized, identically-styled boxes with no hierarchy and a redundant third summary card.

checks.title: Content warrants a diagram over a table/list/sentence
checks.applies_when: Inline skill-authored <svg> diagram present.
checks.spec: Rubric question over the per-figure screenshot + brief: 'Could this diagram's content be conveyed just as well by a short 3-column table, a bulleted list, or a single sentence? Flag diagram-for-diagram's-sake.' Evidence: per-figure screenshot + one-line content brief.
checks.fixture_violation: A three-node A->B->C flow that restates a single sentence with no added structure.

checks.title: Mono-Industrial type budget: ≤3 families, ≤4 sizes, ≤2 weights, no bold sans
checks.applies_when: Mono-Industrial detected: Space Grotesk loaded as --font-body AND no Doto/Libre Baskerville/Manrope loaded (distinguishes from Nothing).
checks.spec: Parse font-family declarations actually applied to elements (first non-generic name per stack only). (1) Distinct applied families ≤3 (Space Grotesk, Space Mono, +optional one Geist Pixel accent); fail if >3 or if >1 pixel-accent face loaded. (2) Geist Pixel @font-face present only if some element's resolved computed font-family (following --font-display var chain) uses it; warn if loaded-but-unused. (3) Distinct applied font-size values (var(--size-*), literal px, one clamp()=one size) ≤4 — display/section/body/caption per tokens.md; error if >4, warn at exactly 4 if the 4th isn't --size-section. (4) No font-weight resolving to 700/bold on any element whose family resolves to Space Grotesk/--font-display sans (check computed style so UA-default <b>/<strong> bold counts); distinct weights in use (excluding Space Mono 700) ≤2.
checks.fixture_violation: Mono-Industrial page that applies a 4th named family (e.g. Inter) to body text, OR sets font-weight:700 on a Space-Grotesk <h1>, OR declares 5 distinct font-size tokens.
checks.fp_guards: Only count first non-generic name per font stack; never count system-ui/sans-serif/monospace fallbacks. Treat clamp() as one size. Space Mono may legitimately be 700.
checks.impl_hint: Resolve --font-display/--font-body var chains before counting; getComputedStyle fontWeight for <b>/<strong>.

checks.title: Mono-Industrial: grayscale only, color is status on values only
checks.applies_when: Mono-Industrial detected (Space Grotesk body, no Doto/Libre Baskerville).
checks.spec: Scan all color/background/border-color/fill/stroke literals. Whitelist documented grayscale values (#000, #f6f4f0, rgba(242,237,229,*), rgba(22,19,15,*) and var(--fg)/--text-*/--rule* forms) plus the six status hexes and var(--ok|--warn|--err). Fail on: any other hue-bearing literal; linear-gradient(/radial-gradient( in chrome; background-clip:text with non-monochrome color or -webkit-text-fill-color set to a hue; var(--ok|--warn|--err) used as background-color/fill on row/section selectors (tr,.row,.section,li) or as color on label-classed text (status marks the value/datum, never a container or label).
checks.fixture_violation: Mono page with a purple accent border on a card, a gradient heading, or a whole <tr> background tinted with var(--err).
checks.fp_guards: Neutral rgba(0,0,0,*)/rgba(255,255,255,*) shadows are legitimate — only flag literals carrying actual hue. Do not flag a small status-colored dot/icon adjacent to a value (routed to mono-status-value-judgment).

checks.title: Mono-Industrial: status color reads as a data event, not decoration
checks.applies_when: Mono-Industrial detected AND mono-grayscale-status-color flagged an ambiguous status-color use.
checks.spec: Rubric (answer from the desktop light+dark screenshots + the flagged elements' text): 'Does every colored element mark a specific value/datum with a genuine ok/warn/err meaning (a value or a small adjacent dot), rather than tinting a container, row, label, or being decorative?' Fail if color reads as decoration or container fill.
checks.fixture_violation: A status color applied as an accent wash behind a section that has no ok/warn/err semantics.

checks.title: Mono-Industrial three-option Light/Dark/Auto toggle with inline-SVG icons
checks.applies_when: Mono-Industrial detected AND profile is scrollable page.
checks.spec: Find one fixed-position container (position:fixed with top/right offsets) holding exactly 3 co-located options whose text/aria-label match /light/i, /dark/i, /auto|system/i. Within it: each option's icon slot is an inline <svg viewBox='0 0 16 16'> with a <circle r='5'> (or proportionally equivalent viewBox scaling), rendered ~12x12; fail if the literal glyphs ○/●/◐ (U+25CB/25CF/25D0) are used as icons.
checks.fixture_violation: Mono page whose toggle has only Light/Dark (2 options), or uses ● ◐ ○ unicode glyphs instead of inline SVG circles.
checks.fp_guards: Require all three labels inside ONE component; unicode circles elsewhere in prose don't count. Scope glyph search to the toggle markup only.

checks.title: Mono-Industrial toggle overrides OS: gated media query + explicit override blocks + localStorage
checks.applies_when: Mono-Industrial detected AND profile is scrollable page.
checks.spec: Sniff the base mode from bare :root token values (dark-first vs light-first). Assert the OPPOSITE-mode @media (prefers-color-scheme:…) block's selector is :root:not([data-theme='<base-mode>']) — not bare :root. Assert standalone :root[data-theme='light'] AND :root[data-theme='dark'] rule blocks both exist and define the full token set. Assert inline script persists to localStorage key 'mono-industrial-theme' via setItem/getItem, and removeItem (falling back to prefers-color-scheme) when the selection is system/auto.
checks.fixture_violation: Dark-first Mono page whose light @media block targets bare :root (so the manual Dark choice can't override an OS set to light), OR missing the localStorage persistence branch.
checks.fp_guards: Detect base mode FIRST — light-first and dark-first invert which query needs the :not() gate. Key may be held in a const; trace it if not a literal at each call site.
checks.impl_hint: Compare :root vs :root[data-theme] --bg values to determine base mode.

checks.title: Mono-Industrial toggle stays monochrome and responsive
checks.applies_when: Mono-Industrial detected AND a theme toggle is present.
checks.spec: Assert the active-state rule background matches color-mix(in srgb, var(--fg) 10%, transparent) (whitespace-tolerant) and active label color references var(--text-display) — never a new hue. Assert an @media (hover:hover)/pointer:fine rule collapses the pill to the active indicator paired with a :hover/:focus-within (or JS mouseenter/focus class-toggle) expansion, with no collapse under pointer:coarse. Assert a responsive rule moves the dock bottom-right with ≥44px touch targets at tablet/phone.
checks.fixture_violation: Mono toggle whose active tab uses a solid blue fill instead of the color-mix monochrome tint.

checks.title: Mono-Industrial Mermaid re-themes on toggle and live OS flip
checks.applies_when: Mono-Industrial detected AND page contains Mermaid diagrams (.mermaid or mermaid.initialize).
checks.spec: In the toggle change handler, assert code that resolves palette via a mode function (checks data-theme then OS preference), clears/resets data-processed on .mermaid, restores stashed original diagram source text, and calls mermaid.run(. Additionally assert a matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)') addEventListener('change',…) triggers the same re-theme when data-theme is unset (live OS tracking).
checks.fixture_violation: Mono page with Mermaid where toggling theme leaves the diagram in the old palette (handler never clears data-processed / re-runs mermaid).
checks.fp_guards: Match on behavior pattern, not a literal effectiveMode identifier.

checks.title: Mono-Industrial token values match the fixed palette spec
checks.applies_when: Mono-Industrial detected.
checks.spec: Extract :root base and data-theme override custom-property declarations. After detecting base mode, string-compare --bg,--fg,--text-display,--text-primary,--text-secondary,--text-disabled,--rule,--rule-strong,--ok,--warn,--err against the exact hex/rgba values in tokens.md for BOTH light and dark modes. Fail on any deviation.
checks.fixture_violation: Mono page whose --ok is set to #22c55e instead of the spec status green, or whose dark --bg differs from the spec value.
checks.fp_guards: Base mode may be dark-first (per mono-industrial.md example) rather than light-first (tokens.md) — resolve base mode before matching each block.

checks.title: Mono-Industrial: zero on-load motion, ≤120ms interactions, instant slide cuts
checks.applies_when: Mono-Industrial detected.
checks.spec: Scan CSS/JS. Fail on: any @keyframes glow/pulse/animated-shadow; any animation applied unconditionally (not scoped to :hover/:focus/.active/[data-*]); any IntersectionObserver toggling opacity/transform on scroll (scroll-reveal); any inline opacity:0 transitioned to 1 on load/DOMContentLoaded; any transition/animation-duration >120ms; any cubic-bezier spring/bounce on non-interaction elements. Require an @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block forcing ~0.01ms durations on *,*::before,*::after. For slide-deck structure, assert slide-changing property (transform/opacity) has no transition (instant cut; scroll-snap handles the step).
checks.fixture_violation: Mono page whose cards fade-up on load via a 600ms opacity/translateY entrance animation.
checks.fp_guards: Allow hover/focus transitions ≤120ms — only flag rules not scoped to an interaction state or exceeding the duration/easing limits.

checks.title: Mono-Industrial Mermaid uses grayscale themeVariables and mono/sans label typography
checks.applies_when: Mono-Industrial detected AND page contains Mermaid.
checks.spec: Parse mermaid.initialize themeVariables: primaryColor/secondaryColor/tertiaryColor/lineColor must resolve to grayscale nodeFill/nodeBorder/edgeColor values (not arbitrary hue). Any classDef setting fill/stroke may only use the six approved status hexes. Assert edge labels resolve to Space Mono uppercase 11px and node labels to Space Grotesk 15px (20px allowed in slide role).
checks.fixture_violation: Mono Mermaid diagram whose themeVariables set primaryColor to a blue hex, or whose edge labels render in a sans font.

checks.title: Mono-Industrial data-row table styling (no zebra, no vertical borders, hairline rows)
checks.applies_when: Mono-Industrial detected AND a real <table> is present.
checks.spec: For <table> elements: no nth-child(even/odd) background-color (zebra); no border-left/right on td/th; row elements carry border-top:1px solid var(--rule); header cells resolve to --font-mono, uppercase, ~11px, var(--text-secondary); cells whose content is numeric (regex \d with %/currency/ratio) are text-align:right with font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums; assert no text-overflow:ellipsis, white-space:nowrap+overflow:hidden, or -webkit-line-clamp on cells (long text wraps, never truncates).
checks.fixture_violation: Mono table with alternating grey zebra rows and vertical column borders.
checks.fp_guards: Numeric-column detection needs cell-content sniffing, not pure CSS.

checks.title: Mono-Industrial label/metadata voice and bracket-notation states
checks.applies_when: Mono-Industrial detected.
checks.spec:
For label/caption/index/metadata-classed elements: font-family resolves to Space Mono, text is uppercase (text-transform or already-caps source), letter-spacing 0.08em–0.14em, not sentence-case prose with trailing colons. Date-like strings in metadata rows match /^\d{4}\s*·\s*\d{2}\s*·\s*\d{2}$/ (no slashes). Empty/error/loading state containers use inline Space Mono ALL CAPS bracketed text matching /^\[[A-Z0-9 .:\-]+\]$/ with no illustrations, multi-sentence

, or fixed-position toast.
checks.fixture_violation: Mono page whose empty state shows an illustrated SVG with a two-paragraph message instead of [NO DATA], or a metadata date formatted 2026/07/04.
checks.fp_guards: Identifying which nodes are labels/metadata vs body prose needs class/context heuristics — restrict to labelled containers.

checks.title: Mono-Industrial spacing scale, card restraint, divider restraint
checks.applies_when: Mono-Industrial detected.
checks.spec: Flag margin/padding/gap px literals not in {4,8,16,32,64,96} and not var(--space-*), excluding 1px hairlines and the toggle's 24px/44px. Flag any card-signature wrapper (border + background-color/fill + border-radius/box-shadow) that contains the page's primary display/hero element (never box the display element), and flag non-table/non-nav border dividers between sibling blocks as spacing-should-have-sufficed candidates.
checks.fixture_violation: Mono hero wrapped in a bordered, filled, rounded 'surface card', or ad-hoc 22px/37px paddings throughout.
checks.fp_guards: High false-positive on one-off icon/radius values — advisory only. Mermaid containers (border-only, no fill) are not cards. Route card candidates that ARE interactive controls (zoom/toggle) to accept.

checks.title: Mono-Industrial code block is a fixed terminal pane in both themes
checks.applies_when: Mono-Industrial detected AND a .code-block / pre>code[class*='language-'] is present.
checks.spec: Assert .code-block background resolves to #0a0a0a in both data-theme states (does not swap with --bg). Assert markup pre.code-block > code[class*='language-']. Assert no stock Prism theme (tomorrow/okaidia/twilight/etc.) is linked — only the custom Mono-Industrial token CSS. Assert no line-numbers/copy-button elements unless requested. Diff-line backgrounds use ~0.1-alpha green/red (never solid alpha=1). Flag code blocks >20 lines not wrapped in <details>.
checks.fixture_violation: Mono code block whose background flips to light --bg in light mode, or that links prism-tomorrow.css.
checks.fp_guards: Insert/delete token class names vary by Prism version — also accept --ok/--err low-alpha as the signal.

checks.title: Mono-Industrial: at most one hero and one pattern-break candidate
checks.applies_when: Mono-Industrial detected.
checks.spec: Assert elements with role='hero'/top-level .ve-hero count ≤1. Count pattern-break candidate signatures (Geist-Pixel-styled elements; border-radius:50% among otherwise rectangular content; spacing gaps ≥2× the largest tier used elsewhere; font-size outliers beyond the declared scale). Flag if candidate count is 0 (sterile) or >1 (noisy) — this is the deterministic pre-filter feeding mono-one-surprise.
checks.fixture_violation: Mono page with two .ve-hero sections, or with zero pattern-break candidates (flat/sterile).
checks.fp_guards: Incidental circular avatars inflate candidate count — final adjudication is the paired LLM pass.

checks.title: Mono-Industrial: exactly one deliberate pattern-break, never via color/icon/gradient
checks.applies_when: Mono-Industrial detected AND mono-hero-and-surprise-count reported 0 or >1 candidates.
checks.spec: Rubric (from the full-page desktop screenshot): 'Does exactly ONE element deliberately break the pattern (an oversized/pixel number, a vast gap, an oversized word, a circle among rectangles, an off-grid slide), and is that break typographic/compositional rather than a color, icon, or gradient?' Fail if zero genuine breaks, more than one, or the break is realized via color/icon/gradient.
checks.fixture_violation: Mono page whose only 'surprise' is a bright accent color swatch, or a page with three competing oversized display elements.

checks.title: Mono-Industrial three-layer hierarchy survives the squint test on desktop and mobile
checks.applies_when: Mono-Industrial detected.
checks.spec: Rubric (from the desktop AND 390px-mobile screenshots): 'When squinted at, do exactly three legible levels of emphasis exist (display dominates, primary reads, tertiary metadata stays quiet), and does the hero stay intact and full-width on mobile rather than shrinking into a card?' Fail if hierarchy reads as flat (1–2 levels), busy (>4), or the hero collapses into a boxed/shrunk element on mobile.
checks.fixture_violation: Mono page where every text block is the same weight/size so nothing dominates, or where the hero number shrinks into a small bordered card at 390px.

checks.title: Named-aesthetic page inverts correctly in both light and dark (contrast + no invisible text)
checks.applies_when: A named aesthetic is detected (Mono-Industrial or Nothing).
checks.spec: Render at 1440×900 in prefers-color-scheme light AND dark (and each data-theme override). In each mode: compute contrast ratio of --text-primary against --bg and assert ≥4.5:1; assert no text-bearing element resolves to a color within ~ΔE small of its own background (invisible-text guard); assert elements spec'd to stay fixed across modes (dark terminal code blocks, CTA-on-blue) do not flip. Fail if any mode has failing contrast or invisible text.
checks.fixture_violation: A Nothing-preset page whose body text is hard-coded #fff so it becomes invisible against the light-mode #F5F5F5 background.
checks.fp_guards: Whitelist elements explicitly spec'd unchanged between modes (CTA text, dark code block) from the invisible-text guard.

checks.title: Both light and dark renders look correct (no leftover wrong-mode colors)
checks.applies_when: A named aesthetic is detected.
checks.spec: Rubric (from the paired light + dark full-page screenshots): 'Do both modes render correctly with no invisible text, no leftover wrong-mode background/fill bleeding through, and no element that clearly failed to invert?' Fail if either mode shows a mode-mismatch artifact the contrast check cannot catch (e.g. a panel stuck in dark colors on a light page).
checks.fixture_violation: A Mono page where one section's background stays dark in light mode because its fill was hard-coded.

checks.title: Stitched page emits each font/library and root token block exactly once
checks.applies_when: Final assembled visual-explainer page (any named aesthetic).
checks.spec: In the final HTML, count occurrences of each distinct @font-face{font-family:'X'} block and each library CDN <script src> (mermaid/chart.js/anime) and its init call; fail if any is duplicated. Count :root design-token declaration blocks defining core tokens (--bg,--fg,--text-*,--rule*,--space-*,--size-*,--ok/--warn/--err); fail if the same core token is (re)declared in more than the sanctioned base + data-theme override blocks (fragment/section CSS must not redefine root tokens).
checks.fixture_violation: Assembled page that includes the Mermaid CDN <script> twice, or a section's CSS re-declaring --bg:… inside a nested :root.

checks.title: Nothing loads exactly Doto + Space Grotesk + Space Mono; Geist Pixel forbidden
checks.applies_when: Nothing detected: Doto loaded AND Space Grotesk body AND no Libre Baskerville (distinguishes from Mono).
checks.spec: Scan <link>/@import/font-family. Allowlist named families = {Doto, Space Grotesk, Space Mono}; flag any other named family, with an explicit hard-fail if 'Geist Pixel' appears anywhere (Mono's default must not leak into Nothing).
checks.fixture_violation: Nothing page that also loads Geist Pixel Square, or swaps body to Inter.
checks.fp_guards: Generic keyword fallbacks are fine; only match named families.

checks.title: Nothing uses exactly three font sizes
checks.applies_when: Nothing detected.
checks.spec: At a single canonical viewport, collect computed font-size across visible text elements, cluster within 1px tolerance; assert distinct-size count ≤3 (one Doto hero, one Space Grotesk body, one Space Mono caption). Nothing states the budget unconditionally — no numeric-hero exception. Error if >3.
checks.fixture_violation: Nothing page with 4+ distinct rendered font sizes.
checks.fp_guards: Measure at one canonical viewport; clamp() fluid sizing must not be swept across breakpoints.

checks.title: Nothing: Doto used exactly once, ≥48px, short text only
checks.applies_when: Nothing detected.
checks.spec: Query elements whose computed font-family includes 'Doto'; assert exactly 1 uses it and its text is short (word count ≤6, not a paragraph/body block). Assert its computed font-size ≥48px (Decision (c): §4.1's 48px is authoritative; treat 36–47px as a warn band due to the §2-vs-§4.1 doc conflict; error below 36px).
checks.fixture_violation: Nothing page using Doto on two elements, or Doto set at 28px, or Doto applied to a body paragraph.
checks.fp_guards: 36–47px is a doc-ambiguity warning band, not a hard fail.

checks.title: Nothing: every label is Space Mono ALL CAPS
checks.applies_when: Nothing detected.
checks.spec: Query candidate label elements (nav a, th, button text, .label/.unit/.kicker/section-kicker classes); assert each has computed font-family including 'Space Mono' AND (text-transform:uppercase OR raw text already all-uppercase).
checks.fixture_violation: Nothing page whose table headers render in Space Grotesk title-case instead of Space Mono ALL CAPS.
checks.fp_guards: Buttons/labels containing dynamic user-supplied mixed-case content (a filename) may legitimately break case — apply to structural/system labels; flag dynamic-content labels for review rather than auto-fail.

checks.title: Nothing accent red (#D71921) painted at most once, identical in both modes
checks.applies_when: Nothing detected.
checks.spec: Count elements where #D71921 / var(--accent) is actually APPLIED (painted) as a foreground, background, or border color across the DOM; assert ≤1. Diff the --accent token's literal value between the dark :root block and the light-mode override; assert identical (red is unchanged between modes).
checks.fixture_violation: Nothing page using red on three separate decorative elements, or whose light-mode --accent differs from dark.
checks.fp_guards: Do NOT count the token declaration itself (appears once per dark/light block) as a use — only count applied/painted uses.

checks.title: Nothing: the single red use is genuinely urgent/destructive/error
checks.applies_when: Nothing detected AND exactly one red use was found.
checks.spec: Rubric (from the screenshot + the red element's text/context): 'Is the single red-accent use an urgent/destructive/error signal, rather than decorative?' Fail if the red is decorative.
checks.fixture_violation: Nothing page using red once, but purely as a decorative underline on a heading.

checks.title: Nothing status colors apply to values only, never labels or row backgrounds
checks.applies_when: Nothing detected.
checks.spec: Assert label-like elements (.label, th, nav items) do not have computed color equal to var(--success)/--warning/--accent. Assert row containers (tr, .stat-row) do not have those tokens as background-color. Confirm .value/.num elements ARE permitted to carry them.
checks.fixture_violation: Nothing table whose entire warning row is filled with the warning color, or a label tinted with --success.
checks.fp_guards: Scope strictly to background-color — a one-off status-colored border is a distinct pattern, not this violation.

checks.title: Nothing: no shadows/gradients/blur/large-radius, no transform or shadow in transitions
checks.applies_when: Nothing detected.
checks.spec: Scan CSS. Fail on: any box-shadow value other than none; linear-gradient(/radial-gradient( outside a hero-vignette-scoped selector; backdrop-filter or filter:blur outside a .nav-dots/magazine-deck-scoped selector; border-radius >16px on .card/card-like selectors (buttons excluded). Also fail on any transition/animation declaration whose properties include transform, scale(, translate(, or box-shadow (hover feedback is border/text-color only). Where an explicit timing is set, durations should be 150–250ms (micro) or 300–400ms (view) with cubic-bezier(0.25,0.1,0.25,1).
checks.fixture_violation: Nothing card with box-shadow, or a button whose hover transitions transform:scale(1.05).
checks.fp_guards: Buttons legitimately use pill radius 999px — exclude button selectors from the >16px card-radius check. The optional hero radial vignette and nav-dots blur are sanctioned.

checks.title: Nothing: no skeleton/toast/spinner/zebra/emoji; bracket-notation states
checks.applies_when: Nothing detected.
checks.spec: Flag class/id names containing 'skeleton','toast','spinner'. Flag nth-child alternating background-color on table rows (zebra). Flag emoji Unicode ranges or multi-fill/filled inside buttons/nav. For elements explicitly marked as empty/loading/error states, flag >1 paragraph and require a bracket-notation pattern /^\[.*\]$/ somewhere in the state's text (e.g. [LOADING…], [NO DATA]).
checks.fixture_violation: Nothing page with a skeleton-loader shimmer, a toast popup, or a zebra-striped table.
checks.fp_guards: Restrict the paragraph-count check to elements explicitly marked as empty/loading/error states — a long help/FAQ block is not an empty state.

checks.title: Nothing buttons: uniform mono spec, one white primary per screen, consistent secondary radius
checks.applies_when: Nothing detected AND button-like elements exist.
checks.spec: For primary/secondary buttons: computed font-family Space Mono, font-size ≈13px, text-transform uppercase, letter-spacing ≈0.06em, padding ≈12px 24px, min-height ≥44px. Count primary buttons (white bg + black text); assert ≤1 per screen. Collect secondary button border-radius values; assert all pill (~999px) OR all technical (4–8px), never mixed.
checks.fixture_violation: Nothing page with two white primary buttons, or secondary buttons mixing pill and 4px radii.
checks.fp_guards: Scope the full spec to primary/secondary variants only — ghost variants intentionally omit background/border.

checks.title: Nothing: exactly one deliberate grid break (excluding structural chrome)
checks.applies_when: Nothing detected. (Static pre-filter detects candidates: full-bleed negative-margin elements, grid-column:1/-1 overrides, oversized Doto blocks exceeding column width.)
checks.spec: Rubric (from the full-page screenshot + the candidate list): 'Does exactly ONE element deliberately break the grid (the single break IS the design), excluding legitimate full-width chrome like nav/footer/section dividers?' Fail if zero breaks (spreadsheet-flat) or more than one (chaotic).
checks.fixture_violation: Nothing page where three different elements each break the grid, or a perfectly uniform grid with no break at all.
checks.fp_guards: The static pre-filter over-triggers on nav/footer/full-width dividers — the LLM must exclude structural chrome.

checks.title: Nothing page shows at least 3 of the 6 signature motifs
checks.applies_when: Nothing detected.
checks.spec: Detect each motif: (1) a Doto-family element; (2) ALL-CAPS Space Mono labels; (3) segmented progress bars (.progress/.seg); (4) dot-grid radial-gradient background; (5) bracket-notation text /\[[^\]]+\]/ inside a dedicated UI-state container; (6) a grid-break candidate. Count detected motifs; assert ≥3.
checks.fixture_violation: A page that uses only the Nothing color tokens but none of Doto hero, mono labels, segmented bars, dot-grid, bracket states, or a grid break (0–2 motifs).
