checks.fp_guards: Restrict bracket-notation detection to dedicated UI-state containers so prose/code brackets aren't miscounted.

checks.title: Nothing: chart series differentiated by more than color
checks.applies_when: Nothing detected AND a multi-series chart/legend exists.
checks.spec: For multi-series charts, check whether series are distinguished ONLY by distinct fill/stroke colors with no accompanying opacity variation, hatch/pattern fill, or distinguishing legend text/shape. Flag color-alone differentiation.
checks.fixture_violation: Nothing bar chart with five series distinguished purely by five accent colors and no other cue.
checks.fp_guards: Single-series charts, or charts where bars are directly labelled/positionally distinct, are not violations.

checks.title: Each slide/page is exactly one viewport with no internal scroll
checks.applies_when: Vertical: at least one section.slide exists. Magazine: a .mag container with .page children exists. Skip entirely if neither (not a deck).
checks.spec: At 1440x900 and 390x844: for vertical decks, each `section.slide` computed height == viewport height within +-2px and computed overflow-y == 'hidden'; for magazine, each `.page` computed width == window.innerWidth AND height == window.innerHeight within +-2px, and both `body` and `.mag` have computed overflow-y == 'hidden'. For every slide/page element assert element.scrollHeight <= element.clientHeight + 2 (no internal overflow), EXCLUDING known nested scroll containers (.mermaid-wrap, .table-scroll, .code-scroll and any descendant whose own computed overflow is auto/scroll). Also assert document.documentElement.scrollHeight <= clientHeight for magazine (no vertical scrollbar). Do NOT run at additional widths — these are fixed-canvas profiles, not responsive.
checks.fixture_violation: A magazine .page with height:120vh (or a section.slide whose child content column has fixed height forcing scrollHeight > clientHeight while overflow is hidden, clipping content).
checks.fp_guards: Exclude documented inner scroll containers (.mermaid-wrap/.table-scroll/.code-scroll) from the scrollHeight assertion; use +-2px tolerance for 100dvh/100vh rounding; measure the outer .slide/.page only, never inner panels.
checks.impl_hint: els=[...document.querySelectorAll('section.slide, .mag .page')]; for each measure getBoundingClientRect + getComputedStyle; nested = el.querySelectorAll('.mermaid-wrap,.table-scroll,.code-scroll').

checks.title: Deck uses the correct single scroll-snap axis for its mode
checks.applies_when: Vertical deck: a `.deck` (or vertical scroll-snap container of section.slide) exists. Magazine: a `.mag` container exists.
checks.spec: Vertical: computed `scroll-snap-type` of the vertical deck container resolves to `y mandatory`. Magazine: computed `scroll-snap-type` of `.mag` resolves to `x mandatory` (or longhand x/mandatory) AND no element in the document declares `scroll-snap-type: y ...` (magazine must not mix in the vertical engine). Use getComputedStyle after load so shorthand/longhand both resolve.
checks.fixture_violation: A magazine whose `.mag` sets `scroll-snap-type: y mandatory` (vertical engine left in) instead of `x mandatory`.
checks.fp_guards: Read computed style (not raw text) so `x mandatory` vs longhand `scroll-snap-type-axis:x` both pass; only flag magazine y-axis when it is on a page-level container, not an inner decorative overflow region.
checks.impl_hint: getComputedStyle(document.querySelector('.mag')).scrollSnapType.includes('x') && includes('mandatory').

checks.title: All deck navigation chrome is position:fixed and layered above slides
checks.applies_when: Any nav-chrome element is present: vertical `.deck-progress/.deck-dots/.deck-counter/.deck-hints`, or magazine `.nav-dots` (or a fixed dot-strip appended directly to body with one child per page).
checks.spec: For each present nav-chrome element, assert computed `position === 'fixed'` and numeric computed `z-index >= 100`. If class names differ, match by structural role (element that is a direct child of body, outside the .deck/.mag, functioning as progress/dots/counter/hints).
checks.fixture_violation: A `.deck-dots` element styled `position: absolute; z-index: 5` so it scrolls away / is covered by an isolated slide.
checks.fp_guards: Match by role when aesthetics rename classes; do not fail merely because an OPTIONAL chrome element (e.g. .deck-hints) is absent — only present elements are checked.
checks.impl_hint: ['.deck-progress','.deck-dots','.deck-counter','.deck-hints','.nav-dots'].flatMap(sel=>[...dom.querySelectorAll(sel)]).

checks.title: autofit text safety-net marker present
checks.applies_when: profile === 'slides' (every slide-deck page, regardless of whether it uses a SlideEngine).
checks.spec: Across combined stylesheet text and inline <script> text, at least one autofit safety-net marker must be present: `fitty`, `autoFit`/`autofit`, `resizeText`, or a `--min-font-size` custom property. Presence-only check; no ordering requirement.
checks.fixture_violation: Slide deck with none of the markers present in styles or scripts (no fitty/autoFit/resizeText usage and no --min-font-size custom property).
checks.fp_guards: Case-insensitive match against the combined styles+scripts text; any single marker satisfies the check.
checks.impl_hint: /fitty|autoFit|autofit|resizeText|--min-font-size/i.test(`${styles}\n${scripts}`).

checks.title: prefers-reduced-motion block neutralizes slide entrance animations
checks.applies_when: The stylesheet defines any slide entrance animation/transition (transform/opacity transition on `.slide`/`.reveal`/`.page`). Skip if the deck declares zero on-load motion (e.g. Mono-Industrial instant-cut decks).
checks.spec: Regex <style> text for a `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block that targets the slide/reveal selectors and forces `transition: none` (and opacity/transform to their resting values) with `!important`. Presence check on stylesheet text.
checks.fixture_violation: A deck with `.slide{opacity:0;transform:translateY(40px);transition:...}` entrance animation and NO prefers-reduced-motion block.
checks.fp_guards: Only require the block when entrance animation actually exists; accept any resting-state neutralization (opacity:1/transform:none/transition:none) not the exact literal rule text.
checks.impl_hint: /@media[^{]*prefers-reduced-motion:\s*reduce/.test(css) && /\.slide[^}]*transition:\s*none/.test(reducedBlock).

checks.title: Slide body text is never below 16px (labels/captions exempt)
checks.applies_when: A vertical or magazine deck is present (section.slide or .page).
checks.spec: For every text-bearing element inside a slide/page, compute getComputedStyle(el).fontSize; flag any resolving below 16px, EXCLUDING documented label/caption/mono-badge roles: `.slide__label`, `.slide__subtitle`, `.slide__kpi-label`, `.pipeline__file`, `cite`, `caption`, table header cells, and any element whose class matches /label|caption|kicker|eyebrow|meta|badge|foot/. Measure at 1440x900 (widest authored clamp min).
checks.fixture_violation: A `.slide--content .slide__bullets li` with `font-size:12px` (not a label/caption).
checks.fp_guards: Exclude the documented small-text classes before flagging; treat clamp() minimums of exactly 16px as passing; only measure leaf text nodes, not wrapper containers with inherited size.
checks.impl_hint: parseFloat(getComputedStyle(el).fontSize) < 16 && !el.closest(LABEL_SELECTORS).

checks.title: Split and full-bleed slides carry zero padding on the slide element
checks.applies_when: profile === 'slides' AND an authoring signal for split/bleed layout is present: a class containing `split` or `bleed` (e.g. `.slide--split`, `.slide--bleed`), or a `data-layout="split"`/`data-layout="bleed"` attribute. (Gated on the markup, not on raw HTML text, so it does not misfire on unrelated `String.prototype.split` calls in a bundled JS runtime.)
checks.spec: For each `.slide--split` / `.slide--bleed`, assert computed padding-top/right/bottom/left are all `0px`. Inner panels keep their own padding — only the slide element itself must be zero.
checks.fixture_violation: A `.slide--bleed` with `padding: 60px` so the full-bleed background no longer reaches the viewport edges.
checks.fp_guards: Check only the outer slide element, never `.slide__panels`/`.slide__panel` children which legitimately pad.
checks.impl_hint: ['Top','Right','Bottom','Left'].every(s=>getComputedStyle(el)['padding'+s]==='0px').

checks.title: Magazine .page has zero outer margin and padding (edge-to-edge)
checks.applies_when: A magazine deck (.mag with .page children) is present.
checks.spec: For each `.page`, assert computed margin (all sides) and padding (all sides) are `0px`. Inner content elements (.page__half, .q, .cell) may pad; only the outermost `.page` boundary must be edge-to-edge.
checks.fixture_violation: A `.page` styled `margin: 24px; padding: 40px`, producing a floating card with a visible gutter on the viewport background.
checks.fp_guards: Only the `.page` element itself; inner half/quadrant/cell padding is expected and must not be flagged.
checks.impl_hint: sides.every(s=>cs['margin'+s]==='0px' && cs['padding'+s]==='0px').

checks.title: Magazine opens/closes dark, has 3+ dark pages, invariant across OS scheme
checks.applies_when: A magazine deck (.mag with .page children) is present.
checks.spec: Classify a page as dark if relative luminance of its computed background-color < 0.2 OR it carries `.page--dark`/`data-tint="dark"`. Assert: first `.page` and last `.page` in document order are both dark; total dark pages >= 3. Then render under prefers-color-scheme light AND dark and assert each dark page's computed background luminance is unchanged between schemes (dark is absolute, not mode-responsive).
checks.fixture_violation: A magazine whose cover is a light tint page, or whose dark cover flips to a light background under prefers-color-scheme:light.
checks.fp_guards: Cross-check the luminance threshold against the `.page--dark`/data-tint marker before failing a deep-navy interior page; compare page-level background, not sub-panel colors.
checks.impl_hint: lum = 0.2126*r+0.7152*g+0.0722*b (normalized); compare across two emulateMedia color-scheme passes.

checks.title: Magazine has >=1 full-bleed stat page with a 100px+ number, never back-to-back
checks.applies_when: A magazine deck (.mag with .page children) is present.
checks.spec: Assert at least one `.page--stat` (or `.stat-xl` primary-number element) exists. For each, assert the primary number's computed font-size >= 100px (measured at 1440x900; authored `clamp(160px,22vw,360px)` satisfies this). Assert no two stat pages are adjacent in document order. Spacing cadence (every 4-6 pages) is NOT enforced.
checks.fixture_violation: A magazine deck with no stat page at all (or a stat page whose number renders at 48px).
checks.fp_guards: Measure the hero number element specifically, not its label; the 4-6 page cadence is advisory only and must not fire.
checks.impl_hint: stats=[...dom.querySelectorAll('.page--stat')]; parseFloat(getComputedStyle(numEl).fontSize)>=100; assert no adjacent indices.

checks.title: No two consecutive magazine pages share a layout type
checks.applies_when: A magazine deck with pages carrying layout-type modifier classes (.page--split/--quadrant/--stat/--dark/--grid) is present.
checks.spec: Classify each `.page` by its layout-type modifier class. For each adjacent pair in document order, assert the layout-type classes differ (split->split is forbidden). Pages with no recognized layout class are excluded from the comparison rather than defaulted to a match.
checks.fixture_violation: Two consecutive `.page--split` pages.
checks.fp_guards: Only compare recognized layout-type classes; skip unclassifiable pages.
checks.impl_hint: types=pages.map(layoutType); for i in 1..n: assert types[i] && types[i-1] ? types[i]!==types[i-1] : true.

checks.title: Interior magazine page tints never repeat on consecutive pages
checks.applies_when: A magazine deck with >=3 interior (non-dark) pages carrying tint via data-tint or distinct background-color is present.
checks.spec: For each adjacent pair of non-dark `.page` elements, compare `data-tint` attribute (preferred) or computed page-level background-color; flag any adjacent pair with an identical value. Dark panels are excluded from the comparison since they legitimately interrupt the rotation.
checks.fixture_violation: Two consecutive interior pages both with `data-tint="surface"` (same background).
checks.fp_guards: Compare page-level dominant tint, not sub-panel colors; exclude dark panels from adjacency.
checks.impl_hint: interior=pages.filter(!isDark); adjacent equal tint => warn.

checks.title: Viewport-filling grid pages used at most once per magazine
checks.applies_when: A magazine deck contains at least one grid page (`.page--grid` or a `.page` whose direct grid tiles/.cell count >= 6).
checks.spec: Count grid pages (`.page--grid`, or `.page` with >= 6 direct `.cell`/tile children). Flag if count > 1. The single-color-block bleed cap is NOT enforced here (no reliable signature — see dropped).
checks.fixture_violation: A magazine with two `.page--grid` pages.
checks.fp_guards: Count only 6+-cell viewport-filling grids, not small 2-3 item groupings inside a split page.
checks.impl_hint: grids=pages.filter(p=>p.matches('.page--grid')||p.querySelectorAll(':scope > .cell').length>=6).

checks.title: Magazine has one-dot-per-page nav strip plus working keyboard paging
checks.applies_when: A magazine deck (.mag with .page children) is present.
checks.spec: Static portion: a `.nav-dots` (or role-equivalent fixed dot strip) exists with exactly one `.nav-dot`/button child per `.page`; inline script registers a keydown handler covering ArrowRight/ArrowLeft/PageDown/PageUp/Space/Home/End that calls scrollBy/scrollTo with a `left` offset. Runtime portion: dispatch an ArrowRight keydown and assert `.mag.scrollLeft` increases by ~one viewport width; click a nav dot and assert scroll position changes to that page's offset.
checks.fixture_violation: A magazine with nav dots but no keydown handler (arrow keys do nothing), or a dot count that does not match page count.
checks.fp_guards: Match dot strip by structural role if class differs (fixed row, one child per page); allow small deltas in scrollLeft due to smooth-scroll timing (assert increase, not exact width).
checks.impl_hint: assert dots.length===pages.length; dispatchEvent(new KeyboardEvent('keydown',{key:'ArrowRight'})); await; assert mag.scrollLeft>before.

checks.title: Slide Mermaid diagrams fill their container and stay legible at distance
checks.applies_when: A `.slide--diagram` containing a `.mermaid` element is present. Skip decks with no Mermaid diagram slides.
checks.spec: After Mermaid render + autoFit: (1) the rendered `.mermaid svg` has no fixed `height` attribute and its getBoundingClientRect().width is within a few px of its `.mermaid-wrap` container width (fills, not tiny). (2) every `.nodeLabel` computed font-size >= 18px. (3) edge path stroke-width >= 2px. Soft signal in the same finding: rendered `.node` group count > 10 (slide budget is 8-10). Also verify the CSS override `.slide--diagram .mermaid svg{width:100%!important;height:auto!important}` (or equivalent) is present as a fallback.
checks.fixture_violation: A diagram slide where Mermaid renders at its intrinsic ~300px width floating in the viewport, with nodeLabels at 12px.
checks.fp_guards: Only run on slides containing an actual `.mermaid`; node-count>10 is advisory within the finding, not an independent fail (hard node cap owned by libraries-poster/mmd-max-nodes-10-12).
checks.impl_hint: svg=slide.querySelector('.mermaid svg'); !svg.getAttribute('height'); labels=svg.querySelectorAll('.nodeLabel'); edges=svg.querySelectorAll('.edgePath path,.flowchart-link').

checks.title: Per-slide-type content counts stay within documented density limits
checks.applies_when: A vertical deck with any of the budgeted slide types is present (content/table/code/dashboard/pipeline/quote).
checks.spec: Advisory predictor of overflow (authoritative gate is slide-viewport-fit-no-internal-scroll). Per slide type, flag when a documented max is exceeded: `.slide--content .slide__bullets > li` count > 6; `.slide--table .data-table tbody > tr` count > 8; `.slide--code .slide__code-block code` line count > 10 (trim leading/trailing blank lines); `.slide__kpis > .slide__kpi` count > 6; `.slide__kpi-val` textContent length > 6 chars; `.pipeline > .pipeline__step` count > 6; `.slide--quote blockquote` textContent length > 150 chars.
checks.fixture_violation: A `.slide--table` whose tbody has 14 rows, or a `.slide--content` with 9 bullets.
checks.fp_guards: Trim blank code lines before counting; exclude thead/tfoot rows from the tbody row count; KPI-value and quote length are soft since autoFit scales them at runtime; report as warn (predictive), never error.
checks.impl_hint: map of {selector: max}; count children; the per-2-lines-per-bullet measurement is intentionally NOT included (flaky under text-wrap:pretty).

checks.title: Section-divider number uses the oversized clamp CSS pattern
checks.applies_when: A `.slide--divider` with a `.slide__number` (oversized decorative number) is present.
checks.spec: Validate the CSS PATTERN, not rendered px (per resolved contradiction: prose says '200px+' but authored CSS is clamp(100px,22vw,260px), which renders below 200px at narrow viewports). Assert the divider number's font-size is authored as a `clamp(min, vw, max)` whose max >= 160px (oversized) and that uses a viewport unit for the preferred term. Fail only a fixed small size or a clamp with a small max.
checks.fixture_violation: `.slide--divider .slide__number { font-size: 40px; }` (fixed, small — not an oversized clamp).
checks.fp_guards: Never assert computed font-size >= 200px (the authored clamp floor of 100px legitimately renders smaller at narrow widths); accept the responsive-height override `clamp(80px,16vw,160px)` inside a max-height media query.
checks.impl_hint: /\.slide__number[^}]*font-size:\s*clamp\([^,]+,[^,]*vw[^,]*,\s*(\d+)px/ with captured max>=160.

checks.title: Decorative SVG accents are used sparingly, not on every slide
checks.applies_when: At least one `.slide__decor` (discrete decorative SVG accent) is present in the deck.
checks.spec: Flag any single slide with > 2 `.slide__decor` accents. Separately, if 100% of slides contain at least one `.slide__decor`, flag (violates 'never on every slide'). Background grid patterns (`.slide--with-grid::before`) are NOT counted as discrete accents.
checks.fixture_violation: Every slide in the deck carries a `.slide__decor` accent (100% coverage).
checks.fp_guards: Count only discrete `.slide__decor` SVG accents, not preset background grid/dot patterns; a per-slide count of 1-2 is fine.
checks.impl_hint: perSlide=slide.querySelectorAll('.slide__decor').length; fraction = slidesWithDecor/totalSlides.

checks.title: No run of 3+ consecutive vertical slides with identical composition type
checks.applies_when: A vertical deck with >=3 slides carrying type modifier classes is present.
checks.spec: Classify each `.slide` by its type modifier (slide--title/content/split/diagram/dashboard/table/code/quote/bleed/divider) as a composition proxy. Flag any run of 3+ consecutive slides with the identical type class. This static run is a candidate only — pair with the composition-variety-visual llm pass for confirmation.
checks.fixture_violation: Five consecutive `.slide--content` slides in a row.
checks.fp_guards: Same type class does not guarantee same visual composition (left- vs right-heavy grids) — this is a warn candidate, confirmed by the llm visual pass, never a hard fail.
checks.impl_hint: detect maximal runs of equal typeClass; flag length>=3.

checks.title: Keyboard hints auto-fade on first interaction or after 4s
checks.applies_when: A `.deck-hints` (keyboard-hint) element is present.
checks.spec: Static pre-check: inline script wires a fade (adds a faded/hidden class or sets opacity to 0) on both a ~4000ms timer and a keydown/click handler. Runtime: on fresh load dispatch a keydown and assert the hints element trends to opacity 0 (or gains its fade class) within one frame; separately, with no interaction, assert the same after 4000ms.
checks.fixture_violation: A hints element that stays fully opaque forever (no timer, no interaction handler).
checks.fp_guards: Check the behavioral outcome (opacity->0 on the two triggers), not a specific class name or mechanism.
checks.impl_hint: getComputedStyle(hints).opacity after keydown; and after a 4000ms wait on a no-interaction load.

checks.title: Dimmed slide text and nav chrome meet distance-legible contrast
checks.applies_when: A deck is present and uses a dimmed text token (--text-dim) and/or fixed nav chrome.
checks.spec: Deterministic pre-filter of the presentation-readability rule: for each slide/page, compute WCAG contrast between the resolved --text-dim color and the slide's resolved background; flag if ratio < 4.5:1. For nav-chrome elements, compute contrast against every distinct slide background (light and dark); flag if any falls below 3:1. Judgment about a single focal point is handled by the separate slide-single-focal-point llm pass.
checks.fixture_violation: A light-background content slide whose --text-dim resolves to #c8c8c8 on #ffffff (ratio ~1.6:1).
checks.fp_guards: Only measure elements actually using --text-dim; nav chrome that uses a backdrop-blur pill should be measured against the pill background, not the slide.
checks.impl_hint: contrast(resolve('--text-dim'), bg); wcag ratio helper.

checks.title: Single-focus slide types read as one clear focal point
checks.applies_when: Screenshots exist for single-focus slide types (title, quote, stat, divider, dark cover/back cover).
checks.spec: Rubric (one question per screenshot): 'Does this slide present one clear focal element, or do three or more elements compete for attention?' Consumes only the per-slide screenshot for title/quote/stat/divider/cover pages. Grid/dashboard/quadrant layouts are excluded (intentionally multi-item).
checks.fixture_violation: N/A (llm pass) — a title slide crowded with a heading, a large diagram, and a KPI row all competing.
checks.fp_guards: Restrict to documented single-focus slide types; do not run on grid/dashboard/quadrant layouts.

checks.title: Review units use the minimum faithful explanatory treatment
checks.applies_when: The artifact is a vertical deck, magazine deck, fixed-stage PresentationDeck, or long-form page with coherent sections. The pass routes every slide or section before judging model fidelity.
checks.spec: pass: pass-operating-model. First route each review unit to none, relational, operating-model, or simulated-surface from its narrative job. Judge only relational, operating-model, and simulated-surface units. Warn when the composition materially hides or misstates a required comparison, sequence, routing policy, state transition, causal/evidence relationship, uncertainty, dependency, feedback loop, or resource flow; also warn when an elaborate diagram is imposed on a unit that needs only a table, sentence, quote, image, or single number.
checks.fixture_violation: A clinician-routing policy rendered as four equal cards with no visible selection rule or separation between mandatory review, representative measurement, calibration, and frontier discovery.
checks.fp_guards: Do not require this treatment on covers, heroes, dividers, quotes, full-bleed images, agendas, FAQs, simple CTAs, single statistics, or one-claim/one-fact units. Lists and genuine peer sets may remain cards. A table, formula, or paired contrast may satisfy a relational unit. Route vague or underspecified units downward rather than inventing an operating model.

checks.title: Consecutive slides/pages genuinely vary their spatial composition
checks.applies_when: The vertical-consecutive-composition-run static check flagged a run of 3+ same-type slides (or magazine consecutive-centered candidates).
checks.spec: Rubric: 'Do these N consecutive slides look spatially repetitive — same alignment and whitespace balance — rather than genuinely varied?' Consumes the screenshot strip of only the flagged run. Confirms or dismisses the static run flag.
checks.fixture_violation: N/A (llm pass) — three consecutive centered slides with identical whitespace balance.
checks.fp_guards: Same type class can still vary layout; only confirm when the screenshots are visibly repetitive.

checks.title: Small lone Mermaid diagram does not float in an empty slide
checks.applies_when: A slide contains exactly one `.mermaid` diagram rendering fewer than ~7 nodes with no branching and no additional sibling content blocks.
checks.spec: Rubric: 'Does this slide read as sparse/empty — a tiny diagram floating in a large viewport with no supporting content?' Consumes the single flagged slide screenshot. If yes, the fix is to pair supporting content or switch to a CSS pipeline.
checks.fixture_violation: N/A (llm pass) — a 4-node linear Mermaid graph alone on an otherwise blank slide.
checks.fp_guards: A deliberately minimalist diagram slide may be intentional; this is warn-level and requires the visual confirmation, never an automatic fail.

checks.title: Deck reconstructs every major point of its source document
checks.applies_when: A source document was supplied for the deck generation (a named source artifact exists alongside the deck).
checks.spec: Rubric: 'Reading the source document and the extracted deck text side by side, list any source item (section, decision card, table row, collapsible detail, footnote) that has NO corresponding slide.' Consumes the source doc text + the extracted deck text (headings/bullets/table rows/cards). Any unmapped major item is a missing-content finding. The naive static item-count proxy is intentionally not used (see dropped).
checks.fixture_violation: N/A (llm pass) — a source with 7 decision cards where the deck only covers 4.
checks.fp_guards: Legitimate consolidation (merging short bullets) is not a drop; only flag substantive content with no representation anywhere in the deck.

checks.title: Poster root is a single element with explicit px canvas size
checks.applies_when: File is a poster TSX source (path matches *.poster.tsx or the poster generate/build pipeline compiles it).
checks.spec: Parse the default-export component's return(...). FAIL if the top-level returned JSX is a Fragment (`<>...</>` or `<React.Fragment>`). Also FAIL if the single root element's className lacks BOTH a `w-[<int>px]` and an `h-[<int>px]` Tailwind arbitrary-value token (or explicit inline width/height in px). The renderer measures exactly this one element and crops to its declared size.
checks.fixture_violation: A poster TSX whose return wraps content in `<>...</>` (Fragment root), or a single-div root that declares only `w-[1080px]` with no height class.
checks.fp_guards: Only the outermost returned node matters; inner Fragments are fine. Accept inline style width/height in px as satisfying the size requirement.
checks.impl_hint: AST: check ExportDefault return ArgumentType !== JSXFragment; regex className for /w-\[\d+px\]/ && /h-\[\d+px\]/.

checks.title: Poster must not use live Mermaid
checks.applies_when: Poster TSX/HTML source.
checks.spec: Grep poster source for a live Mermaid dependency: an import of `mermaid`, a `<script src=...mermaid...>` CDN tag, or a `class="mermaid"`/`<pre class="mermaid">`-equivalent JSX node. Any match is a FAIL. The 1500ms rasterization wait is too short for Mermaid's async render, silently producing a blank diagram.
checks.fixture_violation:
A poster TSX that imports mermaid and renders `
graph TD; A-->B
` inline.
checks.fp_guards: Do NOT flag an `` that embeds a pre-rendered image — that is the documented correct pattern. Only flag live imports/script tags/mermaid-classed render nodes.
checks.impl_hint: Regex: /from ['"]mermaid['"]|src=["'][^"']*mermaid|class(Name)?=["']mermaid/ but exclude matches inside tags.

checks.title: Poster must not access the filesystem at runtime
checks.applies_when: Poster TSX source.
checks.spec: Grep poster source for Node fs APIs: `require('fs')`, `from 'fs'`/`from 'node:fs'`, `readFileSync`, `writeFileSync`, `fs.promises`. Any match is a FAIL — the poster is a headless-Chrome-rasterized bundle with no runtime fs; everything must be embedded at build time.
checks.fixture_violation: A poster TSX with `import { readFileSync } from 'fs'` used to load an asset at render time.
checks.fp_guards: None significant — these identifiers do not legitimately appear in a browser-rendered component.
checks.impl_hint: Regex: /require\(['"](node:)?fs['"]\)|from ['"](node:)?fs['"]|readFileSync|writeFileSync/.

checks.title: Poster fonts declared inside the root element (link + Geist Pixel @font-face)
checks.applies_when: Poster TSX references any custom font family in inline styles/className/font constants.
checks.spec: For each font family referenced by the poster: (a) if it is a Google Fonts family, require a `<link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/...">` (or preconnect+stylesheet) present as a JSX child INSIDE the returned root element (poster has no accessible document head; head-appended fonts won't apply); (b) if it references a Geist Pixel variant ('Geist Pixel Square/Grid/Circle/Triangle/Line'), require a `<style>` `@font-face` block inside the root with matching `font-family` and a `src: url('https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/geist@...woff2')` whose filename matches the variant. FAIL if a referenced family has no in-root source.
checks.fixture_violation: A poster TSX using fontFamily:'Geist Pixel Square' for the hero number with no @font-face block anywhere; or a Google-Fonts family used with the `<link>` moved to document.head.
checks.fp_guards: The 'Doto' substitute loads via a normal Google Fonts `<link>` — do not require the jsDelivr @font-face when Doto is used. Cross-match family name in TSX against the family loaded, don't require faces for system fonts.
checks.impl_hint: Collect referenced families; for each Geist Pixel variant assert an in-JSX <style> contains @font-face with that family + geist@ woff2 url.

checks.title: No poster element overflows the declared canvas box
checks.applies_when: Poster profile; the root declares a w-[Npx]/h-[Npx] canvas.
checks.spec: Render the poster in headless chromium at the declared canvas size. For every content element, read getBoundingClientRect relative to the root; FAIL if any right edge > width or bottom edge > height (element cut by the frame), or if any text node has computed text-overflow:ellipsis with scrollWidth>clientWidth (truncated). This is the deterministic half of the canvas-fit contract; pairs with poster-visual-fit-squint for rasterization-only clipping.
checks.fixture_violation: A poster whose hero headline row is wider than the 1080px canvas so its right edge measures at x=1240 (>1080).
checks.fp_guards: Allow explicit full-bleed background elements marked with a documented `data-bleed` attribute to extend past bounds. Scope to content elements, not the root/background rect itself.
checks.impl_hint: page.evaluate over root.querySelectorAll('*'); compare rects to {w,h} from the declared classes.

checks.title: Poster PNG passes the fit/hierarchy squint test
checks.applies_when: An exported poster PNG artifact exists.
checks.spec: Consume ONLY the exported poster PNG. Single rubric: 'Does the poster read its intended visual hierarchy (one element clearly dominates and the moment-of-surprise / hero number still reads as intentional emphasis), with NO text visibly clipped at any edge and NO large unexplained blank region (e.g. from a grid column that collapsed)?' Answer yes/no with the offending region if no. Catches rasterization-only clipping and fit-rework degradation the DOM measurement misses.
checks.fixture_violation: An exported PNG where the hero number was shrunk to blend in and the right third is empty from a collapsed grid column.
checks.fp_guards: Deliberate generous whitespace in a minimalist design is not a failure — only flag blank regions that correlate with a collapsed layout. Judge from the image alone; do not read the source.
checks.impl_hint: Feed PNG to the vision pass with the one rubric question; no page context.

checks.title: Poster hardcodes one theme, no runtime theme switching
checks.applies_when: Poster TSX source.
checks.spec: Grep the poster component for a live theme toggle: `matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme` driving render output, or `useState` bound to a theme-switch control, or a rendered light/dark toggle button. WARN if present — a poster is a static rasterized artifact; emit two separate posters instead of a runtime switcher.
checks.fixture_violation: A poster component with a useState theme toggle and an onClick button flipping token values live.
checks.fp_guards: A one-time build-time `isDark` read used only to pick which hardcoded constants to bake in is acceptable — distinguish build-time selection from an interactive in-poster toggle (presence of an onClick/setState wired to a control).
checks.impl_hint: Flag matchMedia/useState only when wired to an event handler or rendered control.

checks.title: Poster artifacts use the .poster. filename infix
checks.applies_when: A file was written by a poster build/export command.
checks.spec: WARN if a poster output filename does not match `.*\.poster\.(html|png|svg|pdf|jpg|jpeg|webp)$`. Keeps poster artifacts distinguishable from scrollable HTML output by name alone.
checks.fixture_violation: A poster export written as `q1-recap.png` instead of `q1-recap.poster.png`.
checks.fp_guards: None significant — plain filename convention.
checks.impl_hint: Regex the output path.

checks.title: Poster export waits long enough for heavy async content
checks.applies_when: Poster source loads a slow async dependency (Prism.js over a >40-line code block, animation libs, large Recharts datasets, heavy client JS).
checks.spec: If the poster embeds known-slow async content AND the export invocation has no `--wait-for` value materially above the 1500ms default, WARN of an export race that can capture unhighlighted code / an unfinished chart frame. Prefer pre-rendered tokens/plain `<code>` with inline color spans for Prism inside posters.
checks.fixture_violation: A poster with a 120-line Prism-highlighted code block exported with the default wait and no --wait-for override.
checks.fp_guards: Small code blocks / light posters finish within 1500ms — only flag when a genuinely slow dependency is present.
checks.impl_hint: Detect Prism/anime/Recharts + block size; check export command for --wait-for>1500.

checks.title: Every GSAP timeline created with paused:true
checks.applies_when: Composition JS contains a `gsap.timeline(` call.
checks.spec: For each `gsap.timeline(` call site with an object-literal options argument, require `paused: true`. FAIL if the options object omits `paused` or sets `paused: false`. Autoplay races the engine's seek-based capture and produces nondeterministic frames.
checks.fixture_violation: A composition with `const tl = gsap.timeline();` (no paused) or `gsap.timeline({ paused: false })`.
checks.fp_guards: If options come from a variable rather than an inline object literal, the regex cannot resolve it — flag for manual/LLM follow-up rather than false-failing.
checks.impl_hint: Regex gsap.timeline\(\s*\{[^}]*\} and assert /paused\s*:\s*true/ inside.

checks.title: Timeline registered synchronously, one per composition, key matches composition id
checks.applies_when: Composition assigns `window.__timelines[...]`.
checks.spec: Parse the composition JS (acorn, not naive regex). Assert all of: (1) the `window.__timelines["<id>"] = ` assignment is NOT inside a setTimeout, async function, Promise executor/.then, or a DOMContentLoaded/load listener — it must run during synchronous top-level script execution; (2) each literal composition-id key is assigned exactly once (no duplicate registration); (3) the key string equals the root element's `data-composition-id` attribute (case-sensitive exact match). FAIL on any violation.
checks.fixture_violation: A composition that registers `setTimeout(() => { window.__timelines['reel'] = tl }, 0)`, or registers key 'reel' while the root has data-composition-id='my-reel'.
checks.fp_guards: Use a real JS parser for scope, not brace counting. If the id or key is built dynamically (template var) it is unresolvable — flag as unresolved, not a failure.
checks.impl_hint: acorn walk for MemberExpression window.__timelines; check enclosing scope kinds; count per literal key; compare to root data-composition-id.

checks.title: No nondeterministic or uncapturable APIs in composition JS/CSS
checks.applies_when: Hyperframes composition source (inline scripts + any bundled JS it references; inline <style> for the CSS patterns).
checks.spec: Grep all executable composition JS/CSS for forbidden patterns and FAIL on any live (non-commented) match: `Math.random(`; `Date.now(` / `new Date(`; `gsap.ticker.add(`; native WAAPI `<ident>.animate([` (array-literal first arg); manual `requestAnimationFrame(`; `repeat: -1` and CSS `animation-iteration-count: infinite`; engine-owned transport calls `tl.play()/.seek()`, `video.play()/audio.play()`, `.currentTime =`. Each breaks deterministic frame-seeked capture or hangs the render.
checks.fixture_violation: A composition whose onUpdate uses `Math.random()` for jitter, or a background tween with `repeat: -1`, or code that calls `video.play()`.
checks.fp_guards: Exclude matches inside `//`/`/* */` comments and non-script text nodes. Scope `.play()`/`.currentTime`/`.animate(` to receivers assignable from gsap.timeline( or from `<video>`/`<audio>` element refs; a build-time-only `new Date()` label outside any GSAP callback is flag-for-review, not auto-block. Seeded PRNGs are allowed (see video-seed-literal).
checks.impl_hint: One pattern table with per-pattern regex; strip comments first.

checks.title: Seeded PRNG seed is a literal constant
checks.applies_when: Composition calls a seed-PRNG factory (function name matches /seed/i).
checks.spec: For each seed-PRNG factory call, extract the seed argument; FAIL if it is not a bare numeric literal (e.g. it contains Date.now(), Math.random(), a variable, or a function call). 'Allowed randomness' must stay deterministic across renders.
checks.fixture_violation: `const rand = seedRand(Date.now())` used to place noise particles.
checks.fp_guards: Custom PRNG helpers with unusual names may be missed — broaden the /seed/i name match; only assert on the single numeric-looking argument.
checks.impl_hint: Find CallExpression whose callee name matches /seed/i; assert argument is a NumericLiteral.

checks.title: Composition <video> elements carry muted + playsinline
checks.applies_when: Composition HTML contains a `<video>` element.
checks.spec: For every `<video>` in the composition, assert both a `muted` attribute (boolean or muted="muted"/"true") and a `playsinline` attribute are present. FAIL otherwise — audio must travel via separate `<audio>` for mixing, and unmuted/non-inline video breaks the engine's audio model.
checks.fixture_violation: A composition `<video src="clip.mp4">` with neither muted nor playsinline.
checks.fp_guards: None — direct attribute check.
checks.impl_hint: linkedom: document.querySelectorAll('video'); check hasAttribute.

checks.title: Root composition not wrapped in <template>; sub-compositions are
checks.applies_when: Composition HTML file with a `data-composition-id` root.
checks.spec: If the file is a standalone root composition (no element in it carries `data-composition-src` and it is not referenced elsewhere as a sub-composition), assert the root is NOT a `<template>` and its content is not wrapped in a top-level `<template>`. If the file is loaded as a sub-composition (referenced via `data-composition-src="thisfile"`), assert its content IS wrapped in a `<template>`. FAIL on mismatch.
checks.fixture_violation: A standalone root composition whose entire body content is wrapped in `<template>...</template>`.
checks.fp_guards: Standalone-vs-sub determination needs cross-file context; if it cannot be resolved from available files, evaluate only the resolvable direction and skip the other rather than false-fail.
checks.impl_hint: Check root tagName; scan for data-composition-src references.

checks.title: Video audio routed through separate <audio> elements
checks.applies_when: Composition contains a `<video>` whose source filename does not indicate a silent clip.
checks.spec: For each `<video>` whose src/<source> filename is not a silent pattern (not matching *-silent.*/*-noaudio.*/screen-recording heuristics), WARN if no sibling `<audio>` element anywhere in the document references the same base filename — audio should travel as `<audio>` for explicit mixing, not as a video's audio track.
checks.fixture_violation: A composition with `<video src="interview.mp4">` (clearly has audio) and no matching `<audio>` element.
checks.fp_guards: Genuinely silent clips (Ken Burns stills, screen recordings) correctly have no `<audio>` — do not flag those; warn only, never hard-fail.
checks.impl_hint: Match video base filenames against audio srcs; skip silent-filename heuristics.

checks.title: Composition duration/resolution within format limits
checks.applies_when: Composition root declares data-duration (and optionally data-width/data-height).
checks.spec: Read data-duration/width/height on the composition root. Detect format (reel = portrait 1080x1920 or reel-labeled; long-form = landscape 1920x1080). FAIL (error) if a reel's data-duration > 60 (hard max). WARN if duration is merely outside the ideal band (reel 30–45s, long-form 60–180s) but within absolute limits, or resolution mismatches the format's target — such out-of-range renders are legitimized by an AskUserQuestion confirmation (see protocol).
checks.fixture_violation: A reel composition with data-duration="75" on #stage.
checks.fp_guards: Do not hard-fail merely-outside-ideal durations; only the reel >60s absolute breach is error. The confirmation nuance lives in protocol, not the artifact.
checks.impl_hint: Parse root attrs; branch on aspect ratio; thresholds 60/180.

checks.title: Present reel beats appear in canonical order
checks.applies_when: Reel composition contains beat-labeled sections/classes (.hook/.problem/.context/.mechanism/.proof/.resolution/.cta).
checks.spec: If beat-labeled elements are present, read their data-start values and WARN only if present beats are out of the canonical sequence HOOK→PROBLEM→CONTEXT→MECHANISM→PROOF→RESOLUTION→CTA. Missing or merged beats are allowed (the 7-beat template is explicitly 'a template, not a rule').
checks.fixture_violation: A reel where the .cta section has an earlier data-start than the .mechanism section.
checks.fp_guards: Never hard-fail; never require all beats to be present.
checks.impl_hint: Map beat class -> data-start; assert monotonic among those present.

checks.title: Reel body-beat cut cadence and no reel/long-form mixing
checks.applies_when: Reel composition with multiple clip/scene elements carrying data-start.
checks.spec: Compute deltas between consecutive scene data-start values. WARN on any body-beat delta outside [1.2,1.8]s (excluding the HOOK/CTA first-and-last beats and the MECHANISM beat, which may dwell 3–5s). Also WARN if the delta distribution is bimodal (many sub-2s cuts AND several 5s+ dwell scenes) indicating an accidental reel/long-form pacing mix.
checks.fixture_violation: A reel whose body scenes cut every 4s throughout (long-form pacing in a reel-declared composition).
checks.fp_guards: Suppress deltas inside the MECHANISM beat window and the HOOK(≤2s)/CTA(2s) beats. A single long dwell is not a violation.
checks.impl_hint: Delta series minus mechanism/hook/cta windows; range check + bimodality heuristic.

checks.title: No reel cut lands mid-word of the narration
checks.applies_when: Reel composition with a time-aligned transcript.json (word-level {text,start,end}) present.
checks.spec: Cross-reference each clip's cut data-start timestamp against the word-level transcript. FAIL if any cut falls strictly inside a word's [start,end] interval (±50ms tolerance). Cuts landing in inter-word silence pass.
checks.fixture_violation: A cut at 4.40s while the transcript has the word 'architecture' spanning 4.20–4.75s.
checks.fp_guards: Requires transcript.json aligned to the FINAL cut list; if narration was edited after transcription the check misfires — regenerate transcript after any narration edit. Skip if no transcript present.
checks.impl_hint: For each cut ts, find covering word; flag if start+eps < ts < end-eps.

checks.title: Dissolves/transitions only at beat boundaries, one per boundary
checks.applies_when: Reel composition defines beat-boundary labels (e.g. gsap addLabel matching beat names) and uses cross-fade/shader transition blocks.
checks.spec: Identify full-frame cross-dissolve tweens and registered shader transition blocks with their time ranges. FAIL if any transition sits entirely within a single beat's span (not spanning a boundary), or if any beat boundary has more than one transition stacked. Dissolves/shaders are reserved for beat-to-beat transitions, never intra-beat.
checks.fixture_violation: A full-frame cross-fade tween whose time range sits entirely inside the MECHANISM beat.
checks.fp_guards: A per-element fade-in/out entrance/exit (not a full-frame cross-dissolve between two visuals) is normal kinetic typography — restrict to full-frame/background cross-fades. If beat boundaries are unlabeled/ambiguous, flag for manual review rather than auto-fail.
checks.impl_hint: Locate cross-fade opacity tween pairs / transition wrappers; compare ranges to addLabel boundary positions.

checks.title: Reel captions are a verbatim transcript of narration
checks.applies_when: Reel composition has burned-in captions (.cap elements) and a transcript.json.
checks.spec: Concatenate .cap text in DOM order and diff (word-overlap / edit-distance ratio, ignoring case and punctuation) against the transcript word sequence for the same time range. FAIL any caption segment whose normalized word-overlap with its transcript segment falls below ~90% — captions must be verbatim, never a paraphrase of the visuals. An LLM tie-break may confirm genuine paraphrase vs. acceptable normalization on borderline (85–90%) segments.
checks.fixture_violation: A caption reading 'We speed up queries' while the narration/transcript says 'Our engine cuts p99 latency in half.'
checks.fp_guards: Exclude case/punctuation-only diffs before scoring; minor readability punctuation is fine. Skip if no transcript.
checks.impl_hint: Normalize both streams; sliding-window overlap ratio per caption.

checks.title: Reel caption fits one on-screen line
checks.applies_when: Reel composition with .cap caption elements.
checks.spec: Count words per .cap div. WARN if a 9:16 caption exceeds ~9 words (or a 16:9 caption exceeds ~16 words / 2 lines). Long captions must be split at a natural clause boundary in the script before TTS, never rewritten after the fact (which would break the verbatim rule).
checks.fixture_violation: A 9:16 reel caption with 15 words in a single .cap div.
checks.fp_guards: Word count is a proxy for wrap; cross-check against browser-measured rendered line count before treating as real — hence warn only.
checks.impl_hint: Word count per .cap; branch threshold on composition aspect.

checks.title: Reel caption styled as a legible bottom overlay pill
checks.applies_when: Reel composition with a selectable .cap/caption element.
checks.spec: In a headless render, read the caption's computed style: WARN if font-size outside 36–44px, font-weight != 600, background is transparent/light (not a solid dark fill), the bounding box spans ~100% of frame width (should not be full-bleed), or its box is not within the bottom third of the frame.
checks.fixture_violation: A caption rendered at 24px, weight 400, transparent background, spanning the full frame width.
checks.fp_guards: None major if the caption is reliably selectable by class.
checks.impl_hint: getComputedStyle + getBoundingClientRect vs viewport.

checks.title: Reel content clears phone/caption safe zones
checks.applies_when: Reel composition (portrait 9:16 or landscape 16:9).
checks.spec: At each measured beat/keyframe, compute bounding boxes of visible text/primary-content elements. FAIL if any top edge is above frameTop+safeTop or any bottom edge below frameHeight−safeBottom, using aspect-appropriate margins: 9:16 = 100px top / 200px bottom; 16:9 = 60px top / 140px bottom. Protects against clipping by phone chrome and caption overlays.
checks.fixture_violation: A 9:16 reel headline whose top edge sits at y=40px (inside the 100px top safe zone).
checks.fp_guards: Full-bleed background/decorative art is expected to extend into the safe zone — scope to text and primary-content elements, not backgrounds.
checks.impl_hint: page.evaluate bbox check per content element against margin box.

checks.title: Reel is full-bleed with no gutters or padded frames
checks.applies_when: Reel composition root/stage.
checks.spec: Read computed margin/padding on the composition root/body/stage — assert 0 on all sides. FAIL if any wrapping container introduces a visible border/box-shadow/background distinct from the intended full-bleed canvas (gutters or a padded frame).
checks.fixture_violation: A reel stage with 32px padding and a bordered inner card floating on a background color.
checks.fp_guards: An author-documented intentional letterbox (explicit opt-out comment/attribute) is exempt; otherwise hard-fail per the plain doc rule.
checks.impl_hint: getComputedStyle margin/padding===0; scan wrappers for border/shadow/bg.

checks.title: Reel primary content top two-thirds, captions bottom third
checks.applies_when: Reel composition with distinguishable primary-content and caption layers.
checks.spec: WARN if a primary-content element's bounding box extends below 0.667×frameHeight, or a caption's box extends above 0.667×frameHeight — the two reading zones must not mix.
checks.fixture_violation: A stat headline whose box reaches into the bottom quarter where captions live.
checks.fp_guards: Full-bleed background art is not 'primary content' — scope to text/diagram/stat elements.
checks.impl_hint: bbox y-extent vs 2/3 line per element class.

checks.title: Reel frame has a single focal element
checks.applies_when: Reel composition.
checks.spec: Deterministic pre-filter: at each measured timestamp, count simultaneously-visible (opacity>0, on-screen) card-like content blocks (bordered/backgrounded containers with independent text) above a size/prominence threshold. WARN on any timestamp with 3+ such blocks visible — reel frames should present one focal element, not competing cards. (An LLM may confirm the flagged frame genuinely reads as competing focal points.)
checks.fixture_violation: A reel frame showing three equal side-by-side stat cards at once.
checks.fp_guards: A kicker label + main stat is not two competing cards — apply a size/prominence threshold so incidental labels don't count.
checks.impl_hint: Count visible card-pattern elements per sampled frame.

checks.title: Reel role type sizes/weights within documented ranges
checks.applies_when: Reel composition tags elements with recognized role classes (.hook-stat/.hook-claim/.beat-title/.body-copy/.cap/.kicker).
checks.spec: In a headless render, read computed fontSize/fontWeight/letterSpacing/textTransform per role class and WARN when outside the documented table: hook-stat 180–260px/600–700; hook-claim 80–120px/500; beat-title 60–80px/500; body-copy 32–48px/400; caption 28–36px/600; kicker 18–22px/500 mono ALL-CAPS 0.12em. Enforces the loud, glance-legible hierarchy.
checks.fixture_violation: A .hook-stat rendered at 90px weight 400 (far below the 180–260px/600–700 range).
checks.fp_guards: Role classes are a template convention, not standardized — skip (don't fail) elements with unmapped custom class names.
checks.impl_hint: Per known role class, getComputedStyle and range-check.

checks.title: Reel body copy is not paragraph-length
checks.applies_when: Reel composition with body-copy-classed text blocks.
checks.spec: Count words in each reel body-copy block; WARN if it exceeds ~25 words (beyond ~1–2 lines at 32–48px on a 1080px canvas). Paragraph-length prose belongs in long-form, not a fast-cut silent-autoplay reel.
checks.fixture_violation: A reel body-copy block containing a 60-word explanatory paragraph.
checks.fp_guards: A long single scannable sentence differs from a true paragraph; cross-check with browser-measured line count before treating as real — warn only.
checks.impl_hint: Word count per body-copy element.

checks.title: Kinetic-typography word spans exist in static HTML
checks.applies_when: Reel composition animates word spans (elements targeted by a `.w` GSAP tween).
checks.spec: Assert `<span class="w">` word spans already exist in the final emitted HTML for any element targeted by a .w tween. FAIL if the composition builds these spans at runtime (e.g. textContent.split(' ').map(...) then innerHTML=/appendChild) — Hyperframes captures frame-by-frame and runtime-generated spans can be missing on early frames.
checks.fixture_violation: A composition whose JS splits a headline into `.w` spans at load via innerHTML assignment instead of authoring them statically.
checks.fp_guards: A build/SSR/MDX step that emits static spans into the FINAL HTML is compliant — operate on the emitted HTML, not the source template.
checks.impl_hint: Check emitted HTML for .w spans; flag runtime split→innerHTML/appendChild building them.

checks.title: MECHANISM diagram reveals nodes→arrows→labels
checks.applies_when: An SVG diagram inside a .mechanism beat uses sequential classes matching /^(node|arrow|label)-\d+$/.
checks.spec: Extract GSAP tween start-times targeting node-N / arrow-N / label-N classes. WARN if the staged order is violated: all node-N reveals should precede all arrow-N reveals, which should precede all label-N/mask reveals, so the diagram builds up legibly.
checks.fixture_violation: A MECHANISM diagram whose arrow-1 fades in before any node appears.
checks.fp_guards: Intentional non-linear reveal (e.g. a cyclic diagram) may deviate for good reason — treat as review candidate, warn only.
checks.impl_hint: Group tween starts by class prefix; assert max(node)<min(arrow)<min(label) loosely.

checks.title: Ken Burns pan/zoom scale delta stays subtle
checks.applies_when: Reel applies a GSAP scale tween to still-image wrappers (.photo or similar).
checks.spec: Extract the target scale value in GSAP from/to tweens on still-image elements. WARN if the final scale exceeds 1.15 or the start scale is below 1.0 (delta larger than the documented 1.0→1.08–1.15 band) — large deltas 'look seasick.'
checks.fixture_violation: A .photo Ken Burns tween animating scale from 1.0 to 1.4.
checks.fp_guards: Scope to still-image Ken-Burns wrappers; a deliberately dramatic zoom elsewhere should not auto-fail.
checks.impl_hint: Regex/AST scale target on .photo tweens; threshold 1.15.

checks.title: Inline UI demos embedded as webm video, not gif
checks.applies_when: The page embeds an inline UI demo media element.
checks.spec: For inline demo media, assert a `
checks.fixture_violation: A demo embedded as `` in the page.
checks.fp_guards: Allow an `` gif ONLY when the artifact's target is a documented non-video-inlining renderer (Obsidian/Markdown/LinkedIn preview) — otherwise flag.
checks.impl_hint: Detect data:image/gif inline demo imgs vs video/webm source.

checks.title: Inline demo <video> autoplays, loops, muted, inline
checks.applies_when: The page has a demo `<video>` (identified by a data:video/webm;base64 source).
checks.spec: For every inline demo `<video>`, assert the boolean attributes `autoplay`, `loop`, `muted`, and `playsinline` are all present so the clip behaves like a silent looping GIF-replacement in every modern browser. FAIL if any is missing.
checks.fixture_violation: An inline demo `<video autoplay loop>` missing muted and playsinline.
checks.fp_guards: None — direct attribute presence check.
checks.impl_hint: querySelectorAll demo videos; check the four attrs.

checks.title: Inline demo webm stays within size/length budget
checks.applies_when: The page inlines a demo webm as a base64 data URI.
checks.spec: Estimate the decoded byte size of the base64 demo video data URI. FAIL if > 2MB (inlined data URIs above this make the self-contained HTML sluggish). Additionally WARN if the source clip duration exceeds ~20s (long demos should link externally / use a share link). Fix by reducing frames, fps, scale, or demo length.
checks.fixture_violation: A page inlining a 3.5MB base64 webm demo.
checks.fp_guards: Decoded size ≈ base64 length × 0.75; measure the actual data-URI payload, not the whole HTML file.
checks.impl_hint: Length of the base64 blob after the comma × 0.75; ffprobe source for duration if available.

checks.title: Demo video frame matches the page aesthetic
checks.applies_when: The page embeds a demo video and declares/uses a recognizable aesthetic preset.
checks.spec: Consume a screenshot of the page region containing the demo. Single rubric: 'Does the embedded demo video frame (corner radius, border, shadow, caption chrome) match the page's aesthetic preset and feel embedded rather than glued on?' e.g. Mono-Industrial 8px corners no border/shadow; Blueprint sharp corners 1px slate border; Editorial 12–16px corners subtle border.
checks.fixture_violation: A Mono-Industrial page whose demo video sits in a 24px-rounded drop-shadowed card.
checks.fp_guards: Needs the page's aesthetic as input; judge integration from the screenshot, not the source.
checks.impl_hint: Vision pass on the demo-region screenshot with the one rubric.

checks.title: Demo video conveys motion a static screenshot could not
checks.applies_when: The page embeds a demo video.
checks.spec: Consume the demo frames/video and the feature it illustrates. Single rubric: 'Does this demo convey interaction/motion information that a single annotated screenshot could not?' If not (e.g. only a cursor-move-and-click), recommend dropping it in favor of a labeled still — a low-value embed bloats the page.
checks.fixture_violation: An embedded demo showing only a mouse moving to a button and clicking, with no state change worth motion.
checks.fp_guards: Purely editorial judgment — never auto-enforce as hard-fail.
checks.impl_hint: Vision pass on demo frames with the one rubric.

checks.title: Good/Bad/Ugly/Questions review shows all four categories
checks.applies_when: Page contains a code-review or plan-review block, detected by the presence of at least TWO of the four category headings {Good, Bad, Ugly, Questions} (case-insensitive, whole-word) inside one review section. Skip entirely if fewer than two are present (page is not a review artifact).
checks.spec: Within the detected review section, assert all four category sub-blocks exist with headings matching /^(good|bad|ugly|questions)\b/i. For each of the four, assert it is non-empty: it must contain at least one item/card child element OR the literal case-insensitive text 'None found'. Fail if any of the four category headings is absent, or a present category is empty and does not say 'None found'. This catches silent omission of a category (reads as 'not checked' rather than 'nothing found').
checks.fixture_violation: A page with Good, Bad, and Ugly headings each containing item cards, but no Questions heading anywhere (and no 'None found' placeholder) — three of four present, so the guard fires and the missing fourth is flagged.
checks.impl_hint: headings = dom.querySelectorAll('h2,h3,h4'); match text against /^(good|bad|ugly|questions)/i; for each found category, check nextElementSibling/section content length or /none found/i.
checks.fp_guards: Only run when >=2 category headings co-occur, so ordinary prose pages that happen to use the word 'good' once are never scoped in. Match headings as whole words to avoid 'goodness'.

checks.title: Decision-log cards carry confidence border color and required warning text
checks.applies_when: Page contains decision-log cards, detected by elements whose class matches /decision/ AND that carry a confidence signal (a [data-confidence] attribute or a class of high|medium|low). Skip if no such cards exist.
checks.spec: For each decision-log card, read its confidence level. Assert: high -> computed border-left-color resolves to the ok/green status token; medium -> border-left-color resolves to the blue token AND the card text contains 'inferred' (case-insensitive); low -> border-left-color resolves to the amber/warn token AND the card text contains the substring 'not recoverable' (case-insensitive, fuzzy). Fail on any mismatch between the stated confidence and its required color+text pairing.
checks.fixture_violation: A card marked data-confidence="low" with an amber left border but whose body text omits the phrase 'rationale not recoverable' (or a low card rendered with a green border).
checks.impl_hint: getComputedStyle(card).borderLeftColor; compare to token RGB via a small tolerance; card.textContent.toLowerCase().includes('not recoverable').
checks.fp_guards: Match on the short phrase 'not recoverable' / 'inferred' rather than the full sentence so paraphrase that preserves meaning still passes. Only evaluate cards that expose a confidence level.

checks.title: Fact-check verification summary is complete and internally consistent
checks.applies_when: Output contains a fact-check verification summary, detected by a heading or class matching /verification summary/i. Skip if absent (presence is enforced separately as a protocol obligation, since the engine cannot reliably tell an arbitrary HTML file is a fact-check target).
checks.spec: Within the verification-summary region assert all four data points are present: a total-claims-checked count, a confirmed count, a corrections list-or-count, and an unverifiable-claims mention. Assert every claim-status label used is drawn only from {Confirmed, Corrected, Unverifiable} (no other status words like 'Wrong'/'Partial'). If all three sub-counts and the total are numerically parseable, assert confirmed + corrected + unverifiable == total. Fail on a missing data point, an out-of-vocabulary label, or a count that does not reconcile.
checks.fixture_violation: A 'Verification Summary' block reporting 'Total claims: 10, Confirmed: 8' but with no corrections line and no unverifiable line (missing two required fields).
checks.impl_hint: Scope text to the summary section; regex /total[^0-9]{0,20}(\d+)/i, /confirmed[^0-9]{0,20}(\d+)/i, etc.; label vocabulary check against a fixed set.
checks.fp_guards: Only fires when a summary section exists, so it never flags non-fact-check pages. Reconciliation runs only when all four numbers parse cleanly, so prose-only summaries are not force-failed on arithmetic.

checks.title: Stacked-responsive table cells carry data-label
checks.applies_when: A <table> uses the sub-640px stacked-row degradation pattern, detected by either class 've-table' OR the page CSS containing an @media (max-width:640px) rule that sets a table td to display:block and uses content:attr(data-label) in a ::before. Do NOT run on tables that only scroll horizontally (wrapped in .scroll-x / overflow-x:auto) — those keep normal columns.
checks.spec: For each in-scope table, assert every <td> inside <tbody> has a non-empty data-label attribute. A missing or empty data-label silently breaks the mobile stacked layout (the ::before label disappears and rows become unreadable).
checks.fixture_violation: A <table class="ve-table"> with the stacked-row @media rule present, where one <tbody> <td> is missing its data-label attribute while its siblings have them.
checks.impl_hint: For each scoped table: table.querySelectorAll('tbody td').every(td => td.getAttribute('data-label')?.trim()).
checks.fp_guards: Restrict to tables that actually opt into the stacked pattern (ve-table class or the attr(data-label)/display:block @media signature); never flag plain scroll-x reference tables that keep their columns on mobile.

checks.title: Purple/violet→blue-cyan gradient as a full-bleed background
checks.applies_when: A `<style>`/inline background or background-image uses linear-gradient()/radial-gradient() on a large layout container (body, .hero, section, [class*=hero], or any element sized ≥~40% of the canvas).
checks.spec: Parse gradient background declarations on large layout containers. Convert stops to hue (HSL/OKLCH). FAIL when a 2+ stop gradient sweeps from a violet/indigo band (hue ~255–300) into a blue/cyan band (hue ~195–250) — the canonical purple→blue(→pink) AI-hero wash — OR a straight 2-stop indigo(#6366f1/#7c3aed/#8b5cf6 range)→blue(#3b82f6/#2563eb range) diagonal. Only large background containers count; complements forbidden-accent-colors (literal hexes) and is distinct from forbidden-gradient-text-headings (clipped TEXT).
checks.fixture_violation: `.hero{background:linear-gradient(135deg,#7c3aed,#3b82f6)}` on a full-bleed hero div with no image/data behind it.
checks.fp_guards: Single-hue gradients (two shades of one violet), gradients built only from the page's own documented brand hues, and small chips/buttons are exempt — only the violet→blue/cyan sweep on a large background container fires. 3+ stop intentional brand gradients with non-default hues pass.
checks.impl_hint: Regex gradient() values; convert each stop hex/rgb to hue; test span across the violet→blue bands; gate on container size / hero-ish selector.

checks.title: Ambient blurred gradient orbs / particle backdrop with no content role
checks.applies_when: Document has absolutely/fixed-positioned decorative elements, ::before/::after layers, a particle library (particles.js/tsparticles), or a full-bleed background <canvas>.
checks.spec: FAIL when ≥2 empty (no text/data) absolutely/fixed-positioned shapes combine `border-radius:50%`/large radius with `filter:blur(N)` where N≥20px (or a radial/conic gradient fill), sitting below content in z-order as pure ambient glow; OR a particles.js/tsparticles include or a decorative rAF <canvas> drawing many dots exists solely as backdrop. One restrained on-brand wash is fine; the tell is the repeated copy-pasted glow/particle layer.
checks.fixture_violation: Two `<div>`s: `position:absolute;width:400px;height:400px;border-radius:50%;filter:blur(60px)` in opposite corners behind the hero.
checks.fp_guards: A blurred highlight ring directly behind a real labelled diagram element (selection state) is exempt. A single small palette-matched wash tied to the topic passes — require ≥2 near-identical empty shapes, or a particle/canvas layer disconnected from the subject. Particle motion that IS the content (packets on a network path) never fires.
checks.impl_hint: querySelectorAll for absolutely-positioned empty nodes; test computed border-radius+blur; grep for particles.js/tsparticles and canvas rAF-dot loops.

checks.title: Frosted-glass (backdrop-blur + translucent) as the default surface
checks.applies_when: CSS contains `backdrop-filter`/`-webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(`.
checks.spec: FAIL when `backdrop-filter: blur()` paired with a translucent background (rgba/hsla/hex8/color-mix alpha<1) appears on ≥3 distinct selectors/components — frosted glass used as the repeated default card/nav surface. One deliberate frosted moment (a single sticky nav, or a card genuinely floating over imagery) is a real choice, not slop.
checks.fixture_violation: `.card{backdrop-filter:blur(12px);background:rgba(255,255,255,.5)}` reused on cards, nav, and modal (3 components).
checks.fp_guards: A single purposeful frosted element passes (require ≥3 selectors). Glass floating over real photographic/busy content where the blur meaningfully separates layers is legitimate; the tell is decorative glass over a flat solid color with nothing to blur.
checks.impl_hint: Regex CSS rules combining backdrop-filter:blur( with an alpha<1 background; count distinct selectors.

checks.title: A card nested directly inside another card (double-boxing)
checks.applies_when: Document has ≥2 elements matching a card heuristic.
checks.spec: Card heuristic: border-radius>0 AND (box-shadow≠none OR border-width>0 OR background visibly distinct from parent) AND min bounding dimension >~120px. FAIL when a card element has a strict ancestor that also matches the heuristic and shares near-identical treatment (same class or similar background hue/lightness + radius), i.e. redundant containment rather than two purposefully different components.
checks.fixture_violation:
`
…
` where both carry the same background+border+radius+shadow.
checks.fp_guards: Require the >120px size threshold on BOTH nodes so a rounded chip/badge/avatar inside a real card does not fire. A genuinely distinct inner component (a code block or image with its own subtle corners inside a text card) passes — only same-treatment double-boxing fires.
checks.impl_hint: querySelectorAll('*'), compute the card set via getComputedStyle, then walk parentElement chains for same-set membership + treatment similarity.

checks.title: One-sided thick colored accent stripe (border-left/right)
checks.applies_when: Document has ≥1 card/callout/list-item/alert element with a border or box-shadow.
checks.spec: FAIL when an element has computed border-left-width>1px OR border-right-width>1px in a non-neutral (saturated) color while its top/bottom (and opposite side) borders are 0 or ≤1px — the reflexive asymmetric colored accent stripe on an otherwise plain card. A uniform hairline on all four sides is a different, legitimate pattern.
checks.fixture_violation: `.callout{border-left:4px solid #e11d48}` with no other borders.
checks.fp_guards: Uniform 4-side borders never fire. A traditional typographic blockquote left rule (no card background/shadow accompanying it) is a defensible edge case — still surfaced but only when it carries card chrome; a bare blockquote rule may be downgraded by the reviewer.
checks.impl_hint: Computed-style diff across the four border sides; check saturation of the thick side's color.

checks.title: Reflexive warm cream/sand body background
checks.applies_when: Profile has a measurable dominant body/root background AND preset is not `paper-ink` (paper-ink legitimately owns warm paper).
checks.spec: Convert the computed background-color of <body> (and any full-bleed root wrapper) to OKLCH. FLAG when 0.84≤L≤0.99 AND C≤0.06 AND 40≤hue≤100 — the named AI cream/sand default — UNLESS that hue is within ~20° of the page's own saturated accent hue (cohesive tint, not a reflex). Also surface CSS custom properties named --paper/--cream/--sand/--bone/--linen/--parchment/--ivory as a corroborating signal.
checks.fixture_violation: `body{background:#f5efe3}` (≈oklch(95% 0.02 75)) with a blue accent elsewhere.
checks.fp_guards: Exempt preset paper-ink and any page whose subject is literally paper/parchment/receipts (needs an explicit content cue). Only evaluate the dominant body-level surface, never a small card/badge. If the warm hue matches the page's own brand accent, it is cohesive — pass.
checks.impl_hint: culori (or equivalent) OKLCH conversion on the resolved computed body background; plus regex for the reflex token names.

checks.title: More than four distinct named font families on one page
checks.applies_when: Page has rendered text nodes with resolvable font-family.
checks.spec: Collect the computed primary font-family (first named family, ignoring generic keywords system-ui/sans-serif/serif/monospace) for every visible text node. FAIL when the count of distinct NAMED families exceeds 4. A dedicated monospace stack for code counts as one family, not a violation by itself.
checks.fixture_violation: Heading font, body font, a second display font, a script accent font, plus a mono for code = 5 named families.
checks.fp_guards: Generic fallback keywords never count. One monospace family for code/labels is allowed. Measure resolved var(--font-*) indirection, not raw tokens, to avoid false negatives. Threshold >4 (not >3) leaves room for heading+body+mono+one deliberate display face.
checks.impl_hint: Resolve computed font-family per node via the browser (or CSS parse with var() resolution); dedupe first-named families excluding generics.

checks.title: Training-data reflex webfont as a primary voice face
checks.applies_when: Preset is `custom` (named house aesthetics declare their own type systems) AND CSS declares a --font-body/--font-display or a heading/body font-family.
checks.spec: FAIL when the PRIMARY heading or body family (not fallback, not code mono) case-insensitively matches a reflex-catalog name: Fraunces, Newsreader, Lora, Crimson (Pro/Text), Playfair Display, Cormorant (Garamond), Syne, Space Grotesk, DM Sans, DM Serif, Outfit, Plus Jakarta Sans, Instrument Sans, Instrument Serif. These dominate AI training data for ‘distinctive’ type and signal reflex selection. Warn-only: many are otherwise fine faces; the tell is reflexive default use on a greenfield custom page.
checks.fixture_violation: `--font-display: 'Playfair Display', serif; --font-body: 'Plus Jakarta Sans', sans-serif;` on a custom page.
checks.fp_guards: Only fires when preset==custom — exempts Nothing (Space Grotesk/Space Mono), Mono-Industrial and any preset that mandates its own type. Monospace members used only inside <code>/<pre> are exempt (mono-for-code is legitimate). Any family already present in an existing project tokens file passes (identity preservation). IBM Plex / Space Mono deliberately excluded from the name list to avoid fighting code stacks and the Nothing preset.
checks.impl_hint: CSS parse (not naive search) of font-family/--font-* with var() resolution; word-boundary case-insensitive match against the reflex list; scope to preset==custom.

checks.title: Adjacent type roles differ too little to establish hierarchy
checks.applies_when: Page exposes at least two distinct type roles (a largest heading and body copy) that are not part of a dense data table.
checks.spec: Read computed font-size for the largest heading and for body copy (and, where present, adjacent heading levels h1/h2/h3). Compute adjacent ratios. FLAG when an adjacent pair's size ratio is <1.25 AND font-weight is equal AND color is near-identical — i.e. no dimension of hierarchy (size, weight, or color) meaningfully separates the roles. This is the mechanical complement to the llm-pass hierarchy-squint-test.
checks.fixture_violation: h1 18px/600 #111, body 16px/600 #111 — ratio 1.13, same weight, same color.
checks.fp_guards: Pages that carry hierarchy deliberately via weight or color (not size) pass — only flag when size AND weight AND color all fail to differ. Skip dense data tables / compact dashboards where close ratios are intentional density. Measure clamp() at one reference viewport, not per breakpoint.
checks.impl_hint: Computed font-size/weight/color for role elements; adjacent-ratio arithmetic; require all three dimensions to collapse before flagging.

checks.title: All-caps label with no letter-spacing
checks.applies_when: Preset is `custom` or unset (named aesthetics that mandate ALL-CAPS mono labels own their own tracking) AND the page has short uppercase label-like text.
checks.spec: Find short (≤~6 word) label-like elements with computed text-transform:uppercase (eyebrows, badges, nav labels, kickers) whose computed letter-spacing is `normal` or 0. FLAG — untracked all-caps reads as an unstyled default; capitals need ~0.05–0.12em tracking.
checks.fixture_violation: `.badge{text-transform:uppercase}` rendering `BETA` with default letter-spacing.
checks.fp_guards: Exempt preset pages (Mono-Industrial/Nothing mandate all-caps mono labels and set their own tracking) and .section-label/.kicker patterns whose tracking is preset-defined. Only short label text — never incidental all-caps acronyms in running prose. Warn (some fonts carry generous built-in cap spacing that can't be detected mechanically).
checks.impl_hint: Computed text-transform + letter-spacing on short label nodes; scope to preset==custom; exempt .section-label/.kicker.

checks.title: clamp() type with runaway max size / ratio
checks.applies_when: CSS uses `clamp(` on a font-size for a prose/heading role.
checks.spec: Parse clamp(min,preferred,max) on font-size for body/heading roles; normalize min/max to one unit. FLAG when max/min > ~2.5 (breaks zoom/reflow) OR when max exceeds ~6rem/96px on a hero/display heading (shouting, not designing).
checks.fixture_violation: `font-size:clamp(1rem, 5vw, 4rem)` on body prose (ratio 4.0); or `clamp(2rem,8vw,9rem)` on h1.
checks.fp_guards: Exempt slide section-divider numbers (owned by divider-number-oversized-clamp) and poster/video-comp fixed canvases where a type-specimen scale is the point. A deliberate single-word poster specimen may exceed 6rem — warn, not error.
checks.impl_hint: Regex clamp() args on font-size rules; unit-normalize; test ratio and absolute max; skip divider/poster selectors.

checks.title: Webfont loaded without font-display: swap
checks.applies_when: Page loads a custom webfont via @font-face or a Google Fonts <link>/@import (system-font-only pages exempt).
checks.spec: The skill MANDATES Google Fonts <link>s — this check never bans the link, it only requires non-blocking load. FLAG @font-face blocks missing `font-display` entirely (defaults to auto/block → FOIT) and Google-Fonts <link>/@import URLs missing `&display=swap`.
checks.fixture_violation: `<link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Foo">` with no `&display=swap`.
checks.fp_guards: System-font-stack pages (no @font-face/webfont link) never fire. Posters are exempt (fonts load inside the root before a single export render, FOIT is irrelevant) — hence page/slides/magazine only. Only require display=swap|optional; do not flag the link itself.
checks.impl_hint: Regex @font-face for font-display; regex Google Fonts URLs for display=swap.

checks.title: Viewport meta disables pinch-zoom
checks.applies_when: Document has a `<meta name="viewport">` tag.
checks.spec: FAIL when the viewport meta content contains `user-scalable=no` or `maximum-scale=1(.0)?` — an accessibility-breaking app-boilerplate default that never belongs on a static explainer/slide/magazine page.
checks.fixture_violation: `<meta name=viewport content="width=device-width,initial-scale=1,user-scalable=no">`.
checks.fp_guards: None needed beyond scope: poster/video-comp fixed canvases (no user zoom surface) are excluded via profiles.
checks.impl_hint: Regex the viewport meta content string.

checks.title: Too many saturated hues in decorative chrome
checks.applies_when: Page uses ≥5 elements with saturated (non-neutral) fills/backgrounds/borders outside charts and code.
checks.spec: Collect rendered colors with OKLCH chroma>0.08 (drop near-neutrals) used in decorative/UI chrome — badges, icons, section accents, headings, card borders, buttons. Bin hues into 30° buckets. FLAG when >4 distinct buckets appear. Distinct from forbidden-accent-colors (specific hexes); this caps overall hue variety.
checks.fixture_violation: Badges/section accents in red, orange, green, blue, and purple with no data-encoding rationale.
checks.fp_guards: Exempt data-visualization categorical scales (chart series/legends), syntax highlighting, and semantic status colors — those legitimately need many distinguishable hues. Only chrome-level decorative color counts.
