checks.impl_hint: Collect computed colors from chrome elements; OKLCH chroma filter; 30° hue-bucket count; exclude chart/legend/code containers.

checks.title: Neutral gray text on a saturated background
checks.applies_when: Page has text sitting on a saturated (non-neutral) background surface.
checks.spec: For each text node, resolve the nearest non-transparent ancestor background. When that background's OKLCH chroma >~0.05 (a real accent color) and the text color is a mid-tone (L 0.4–0.7) near-zero-chroma gray — not a tint/shade of the background hue, not near-white/near-black at reduced alpha — FLAG as washed-out.
checks.fixture_violation: `.badge{background:#2563eb;color:#6b7280}` (mid-gray on a blue badge).
checks.fp_guards: Gray text on a near-neutral/off-white surface is unaffected (only saturated backgrounds count). Intentional near-white/near-black at sufficient contrast passes — the failure mode is specifically mid-gray on an accent color.
checks.impl_hint: Convert text + resolved background to OKLCH; compare hue alignment and the text-lightness band.

checks.title: Body/secondary text fails WCAG AA contrast
checks.applies_when: Page renders informational text nodes with a resolvable effective background.
checks.spec: For each informational text node, resolve effective text color and the first opaque stacked-ancestor background; compute the WCAG relative-luminance contrast ratio. FAIL when font-size<24px (or <18.66px bold) with ratio<4.5:1, or ≥24px/≥18.66px-bold with ratio<3:1. Test both light and dark renders. Catches the pale-elegant-gray failure that is the single biggest reason AI designs read as hard to read.
checks.fixture_violation: `body{color:#9aa0a6;background:#fff}` primary paragraph text (≈3.3:1).
checks.fp_guards: Exempt aria-hidden/decorative/watermark text and disabled form controls. Intentional low-emphasis microcopy that is genuinely secondary (small, clearly de-emphasized) may sit near 3:1 and is judged leniently; the flag targets primary reading content and mis-set muted labels.
checks.impl_hint: Per text node: computed color + stacked-resolved background → standard WCAG contrast formula; run under both color schemes.

checks.title: outline removed with no :focus-visible replacement
checks.applies_when: Document has focusable/interactive elements (a[href], button, input, [tabindex], summary).
checks.spec: FAIL when `outline: none|0` is applied to a focusable selector (a/button/input/[tabindex]/summary) or a global reset (*, :focus) AND no overlapping `:focus-visible` (or `:focus`) rule supplies a visible replacement (a non-none outline, a contrasting box-shadow ring, or a border/background change).
checks.fixture_violation: `a,button{outline:none}` with no :focus-visible rule anywhere.
checks.fp_guards: If a real :focus-visible replacement with visible contrast exists for that selector family, pass. Pages with zero focusable/interactive elements (pure static diagram/poster) never apply — hence page/slides/magazine scope.
checks.impl_hint: Collect selectors setting outline:none; cross-check for an overlapping :focus-visible/:focus rule with a visible property.

checks.title: One identical fade-and-rise reveal on most/every section
checks.applies_when: Page uses scroll/load-triggered entrance animation (a reveal class toggled by IntersectionObserver, `animation-timeline: view()`, or per-region load @keyframes).
checks.spec: FLAG when one shared entrance rule of the form `opacity:0; transform:translateY(N)` → `opacity:1; transform:none` (or a single .reveal/.fade-in-up class) is applied to ≥60% of top-level sections/body children, OR when >3 unrelated structural regions (header, several sections, footer) each carry their own load-triggered staggered entrance. The saturated AI motion default is the same reveal looped down the page.
checks.fixture_violation: Every `<section>` carries `class="reveal"` with one shared translateY fade toggled on scroll.
checks.fp_guards: One deliberate hero-only reveal, or 2–3 sections each animated differently (varied direction/timing/property), is legitimate pacing and passes. Staggering siblings WITHIN one list/grid is fine — only cross-section uniformity fires. Pages with ≤2 sections never trigger.
checks.impl_hint: Identify the reveal rule/class; count carrying sections vs total top-level sections; ratio≥0.6 or >3 unrelated regions.

checks.title: Content hidden by default, revealed only via JS, no fallback
checks.applies_when: CSS sets `opacity:0` or `visibility:hidden` on a content selector that a script later reveals.
checks.spec: FAIL when a class/selector defaults content to opacity:0 / visibility:hidden and a companion selector (same base + .in-view/.visible/.revealed/.active) restores it via JS `classList.add`, AND there is neither an `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` rule forcing the visible state nor a <noscript> override. Headless renders, PDF/PNG export, and print skip the scroll/JS trigger, so the section ships blank — a correctness failure, not just a motion tell. Highest-value check for exported static artifacts.
checks.fixture_violation: `.section{opacity:0}` + JS `IntersectionObserver` adding `.visible{opacity:1}`, with no reduced-motion or noscript fallback.
checks.fp_guards: CSS-only `animation-timeline: view()` scroll-driven reveals (no JS dependency, degrade to visible) never fire. Content that is already visible and only gets added movement/emphasis on top passes — only JS-class-gated invisibility with no fallback fails.
checks.impl_hint: Match opacity:0/visibility:hidden selectors to a .revealed-suffix companion + classList.add in script; assert absence of reduced-motion/noscript fallback.

checks.title: Decorative motion animates layout properties
checks.applies_when: CSS has a transition/@keyframes affecting geometry.
checks.spec: FLAG transition/@keyframes that animate width, height, top, left, right, bottom, or plain margin/padding for decorative or continuous/looping motion instead of transform/opacity. Compositor-friendly transform:translate/scale should carry motion.
checks.fixture_violation: `@keyframes drift{from{left:0}to{left:40px}}` looped on a decorative element.
checks.fp_guards: grid-template-rows (0fr→1fr) and a single short (<150ms) bounded max-height disclosure toggle are accepted FLIP-style exceptions. Only flag decorative/continuous motion on a non-trivial box, not a 1–2px functional nudge.
checks.impl_hint: Regex transition-property / @keyframes step declarations for width/height/top/left/right/bottom/margin/padding changes; exempt grid-template-rows and short max-height toggles.

checks.title: Bounce/elastic overshoot easing on standard motion
checks.applies_when: CSS/JS declares a cubic-bezier() timing function or a named easing.
checks.spec: FAIL when any cubic-bezier(x1,y1,x2,y2) has y1<0/y1>1 or y2<0/y2>1 (mathematical overshoot signature), or an animation-name/timing-function/JS ease identifier matches /bounce|elastic|spring/i, applied to standard reveal/hover/tooltip/entrance motion.
checks.fixture_violation: `transition-timing-function: cubic-bezier(0.34,1.56,0.64,1)` on a card hover.
checks.fp_guards: The skill's prescribed curves — cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1), (0.22,1,0.36,1), (0.16,1,0.3,1) — keep all control points in [0,1] and pass. Named aesthetics with zero motion (Mono-Industrial) never trigger. A deliberate physical-toy bounce would be a rare, explicitly-commented exception, not the default.
checks.impl_hint: Parse cubic-bezier args and range-check y values; regex bounce/elastic/spring identifiers.

checks.title: Interactive control smaller than 44x44px
checks.applies_when: Artifact renders clickable navigation/controls (button, a[href], [role=button], [onclick], input) — e.g. slide-nav dots, tab buttons, accordion toggles.
checks.spec: Measure getBoundingClientRect() for each interactive control. FLAG any with rendered width<44 OR height<44. Fires only where interactive elements actually exist.
checks.fixture_violation: Slide-nav dots rendered at 16x16px with no padded hit area.
checks.fp_guards: Purely illustrative icons not wrapped in a clickable element are not measured. Most static diagrams/posters have no interactive controls and never trigger. A small visible glyph is fine if its clickable parent provides a ≥44px hit area via padding.
checks.impl_hint: Per interactive element getBoundingClientRect; compare against 44px.

checks.title: Spinner/skeleton/progress in a static artifact that fetches nothing
checks.applies_when: Document contains a loading affordance (class/id ~ skeleton|spinner|loading|progress-bar, or a [role=progressbar]).
checks.spec: FLAG when a skeleton/spinner/progress element plays once via setTimeout or CSS animation-delay on load in a file that contains no `fetch(`, `XMLHttpRequest`, or dynamic `import(` — i.e. there is no real async work to wait for, so the loading sequence is pure theater.
checks.fixture_violation: A `.skeleton` shimmer that fades to real content after a 1200ms setTimeout, in a file with no fetch.
checks.fp_guards: If the artifact genuinely streams/lazy-loads (fetch, XHR, dynamic import, lazy images) the loading UI is functional — do not flag. Only timer-driven theater with no matching async operation fires.
checks.impl_hint: Detect skeleton/spinner/progress element + timed reveal; assert no fetch/XHR/dynamic-import in the file.

checks.title: Inconsistent icon systems for the same repeated role
checks.applies_when: Page has a repeated list/row/section pattern with leading markers or icons on ≥3 same-level items.
checks.spec: For same-level, same-role sibling markers (leading glyphs before list items or section labels), classify each as {emoji, unicode-symbol, inline-svg, icon-font, none}. FLAG when siblings serving the identical role mix ≥2 categories with no rule for which is used where.
checks.fixture_violation: A stat list where row 1 uses an emoji, row 2 an inline <svg>, row 3 a `▸` unicode arrow.
checks.fp_guards: Different marker kinds for genuinely different semantic categories (a green check vs a red X) are not inconsistency. Only flag mixed systems among same-level equivalent items.
checks.impl_hint: Collect leading markers per sibling group; classify token kind; flag >1 kind within a role group.

checks.title: Emoji used as inline list/paragraph bullets
checks.applies_when:
Page has ≥3
  • /

    items.
checks.spec: FLAG a leading emoji codepoint (U+1F300–U+1FAFF, U+2600–U+27BF, U+2B00–U+2BFF, U+FE0F) followed by ASCII text recurring across ≥3 list items or paragraphs (the '✅ Fast / 🚀 Scalable / 💡 Note' pattern). Distinct from no-emoji-in-ui-chrome, which covers headers/status/table cells, not body bullets.
checks.fixture_violation: A `<ul>` whose items read `✅ Fast`, `🚀 Scalable`, `🔒 Secure`.
checks.fp_guards: A one-off meaningful emoji (a flag glyph naming a country in real data, a status glyph inside an actual UI mock) is exempt — require the decorative pattern to recur across ≥3 items. Emoji inside <code>/<pre> as literal content is exempt.
checks.impl_hint:
Walk
  • /

    text nodes; test leading-emoji-then-text regex; tally per document.

checks.title: Color/icon-only meaning with no text label or legend
checks.applies_when: Page has a red/green contrastive status pair, or a legend/key row of bare swatches/icons.
checks.spec: FLAG when (a) status/comparison meaning is carried by a red-band vs green-band color pair used side by side with no status word (pass/fail/before/after/old/new/added/removed/error/success/deprecated), no icon child, and no legend mapping color to meaning within ~100px; OR (b) a legend/key container (class/id ~ legend|key, or a row of small fixed-size swatches/icons) has swatches/icons with no adjacent text, title, or aria-label.
checks.fixture_violation: Two side-by-side cells, one red one green, with no words; or a legend row of three bare colored dots.
checks.fp_guards: A single consistent brand accent used site-wide for links never fires. When every swatch already has an adjacent label (the correct pattern) it passes. A single universally-unambiguous icon (a close ‘×’) is fine.
checks.impl_hint: Hue-band DOM scan for contrastive red/green without status text; legend-container scan for swatch labels/title/aria.

checks.title: One generic box-shadow copy-pasted onto every raised element
checks.applies_when: CSS declares ≥5 box-shadow rules across different selectors.
checks.spec: FLAG when a single box-shadow value (or a family within ~10% blur/spread/alpha tolerance) is reused verbatim across ≥5 structurally different element types (e.g. card AND button AND image AND code block AND nav) — generic 'depth' with no elevation logic.
checks.fixture_violation: `0 4px 6px rgba(0,0,0,.1)` applied identically to cards, buttons, images, code blocks, and nav.
checks.fp_guards: A deliberate one-token elevation applied consistently to ONE role (only clickable cards) is legitimate system reuse. Only flag when the same shadow spans unrelated element types.
checks.impl_hint: Extract box-shadow declarations; cluster near-identical values; count distinct selector element-types sharing one value.

checks.title: Uniform lowercase descriptor gloss on every repeated item
checks.applies_when: Page has a grid/list/catalog of 5+ structurally-similar siblings.
checks.spec: Warn when >=5 sibling items each pair a label with a 1-4 word lowercase non-sentence gloss in muted styling, e.g. `collapsible data`, `one canvas`, `titled region`. One deliberate descriptor is voice; a gloss on every item is template grammar.
checks.fixture_violation: A five-item feature grid where every item has a title plus a muted lowercase two-word tagline.
checks.fp_guards: Exempt tables, units, dates, prices, form hints, and stat/metric labels.
checks.impl_hint: Scan direct sibling groups; require >=5 label+muted lowercase-gloss pairs and skip table/form/stat/metric contexts plus unit/date/price text.

checks.title: Repeated big-number stat tiles as decorative filler
checks.applies_when: Page has ≥3 sibling tiles each pairing a large numeric node with a short label.
checks.spec: Structurally find a repeated sibling group (≥3) where each item contains a >70%-digit/percent/currency node at computed font-size ≥~2.5x body size plus a much smaller ≤~4-word label, with identical structure/classing and a decorative gradient/colored accent repeated across all tiles.
checks.fixture_violation: Three identical tiles: `99.9%` / `10x` / `24/7` big numbers over small labels on a gradient bar, unsourced.
checks.fp_guards: Exempt tiles whose numbers are traceable to the artifact's real source (an actual diff line count, a genuine dataviz KPI) and stat grids where each tile's accent is meaningfully data-driven rather than one repeated decorative gradient. The data-traceability judgment is deferred to the copy/completeness reviewer; the structural signature is the mechanical prefilter.
checks.impl_hint: Structural-similarity scan across sibling subtrees; digit-ratio + font-size test on the numeral node; sameness test on the accent.

checks.title: Every content block boxed in card chrome by default
checks.applies_when: Main content flow has ≥4 top-level content blocks.
checks.spec: FLAG when ≥90% of top-level content blocks are wrapped in card chrome (border-radius>0 + border/shadow/distinct background) AND at least one such card wraps a single unstructured item (one paragraph/image/stat) with no structurally-similar repeated siblings — boxing arbitrary singular content out of habit rather than reserving cards for distinct repeated units.
checks.fixture_violation: A lone intro paragraph, a single image, and a lone stat each wrapped in its own bordered/shadowed card.
checks.fp_guards: A genuine grid of distinct actionable items (pricing tiers, module links) legitimately cards every item — that is the separate uniform-card-grid pattern, not this. Only habitual boxing of singular non-repeating content fires.
checks.impl_hint: Count top-level blocks vs card-wrapped fraction; check for a card wrapping a single item with no sibling twins.

checks.title: A single flat list of 8+ undifferentiated items
checks.applies_when: Page has a top-level <ul>/<ol> with ≥8 direct <li> children.
checks.spec: FLAG a top-level list with ≥8 direct <li> children where no sub-heading-weight element (strong/b/h5/h6 opening a subgroup) and no divider (hr/border-top) breaks it into clusters, AND items are multi-clause explanatory sentences (avg >~60 chars) — a bullet dump that should have been grouped or turned into a table/diagram.
checks.fixture_violation: A flat 10-item `<ul>` of full-sentence explanations with no grouping.
checks.fp_guards: Genuinely flat enumerable data (a glossary, an index, a one-line-per-entry changelog) is correct as a flat list and passes — gate on average item length so short data rows don't fire.
checks.impl_hint: Count direct <li> of top-level lists; check for subgroup markers/dividers and average textContent length.

checks.title: Spacing values not drawn from a shared scale
checks.applies_when: CSS declares ≥8 distinct px margin/padding/gap/inset values.
checks.spec: Extract raw px margin/padding/gap/inset literals from authored CSS + inline styles. FLAG when the fraction NOT divisible by a common base (test 4 and 8) exceeds ~30% AND there are ≥8 distinct values — hand-picked one-off spacing (13/17/22/29px) instead of a scale.
checks.fixture_violation: margin/padding values of 13px, 17px, 22px, 27px, 33px scattered with no 4/8 base.
checks.fp_guards: Count only hardcoded px literals, not rem/em/clamp() resolved results (those land on odd pixels legitimately). One to three deliberate optical-nudge exceptions are expected and don't trip the fraction threshold.
checks.impl_hint: Regex px spacing literals; test divisibility by 4/8; ratio + distinct-count thresholds.

checks.title: Mixed Title Case / Sentence case at one heading level
checks.applies_when: Page has ≥3 headings at the same level (or ≥3 same-class card titles).
checks.spec: For each heading level / repeated title component, classify each heading as Title Case, Sentence case, or ALL CAPS. FLAG when a single level mixes ≥2 casing styles across ≥3 headings — casing drift from section-by-section drafting.
checks.fixture_violation: H2s reading `Getting Started`, `How it works`, `Deploy Your App` (Title + Sentence mixed).
checks.fp_guards: A deliberate two-tier convention (all-caps eyebrow above sentence-case title) is a different structural level, not a violation — only compare genuinely same-level headings. Preserve intentional brand casing.
checks.impl_hint: Group headings by level/component; classify casing; flag ≥2 styles within a level.

checks.title: Two fonts paired within the same typographic classification
checks.applies_when: Page uses two different (non-code) font families for heading vs body.
checks.spec: pass: pass-visual-tells. Rubric question: 'Do the heading and body fonts fall in the same typographic classification (both geometric sans, both humanist sans, both transitional serif — e.g. Poppins+Montserrat, Inter+Manrope, DM Sans+Roboto) and provide no real contrast axis?' YES = warn. One family in multiple weights, or a genuine contrast pairing (serif+sans, mono+sans, condensed display+wide body), passes.
checks.fixture_violation: Heading in Poppins, body in Montserrat — two near-identical geometric sans presented as a 'pairing'.
checks.fp_guards: A single family across weights is fine. Only flag two DIFFERENT families in the same bucket with no discernible contrast; genuine cross-axis pairings pass.

checks.title: Sole accent is a reflexive blue/orange default
checks.applies_when: Page has a single dominant saturated accent color and no documented brand palette in source.
checks.spec: pass: pass-visual-tells. Rubric question: 'Is the only meaningfully-saturated accent hue a reflexive default — indigo/blue near hue 245–265 or warm orange near hue 45–65 — reached for without any brand reason, rather than a color justified by the artifact's actual subject or brand?' YES = warn.
checks.fixture_violation: A page whose sole accent is #6366f1 indigo with no brand rationale.
checks.fp_guards: If source material establishes blue/orange as a real brand identity (existing logo/brand assets), it is not a reflex — pass. Judgment call, warn only.

checks.title: Heading restates its own body, or a point repeats across sections
checks.applies_when: Page has section headings each followed by prose.
checks.spec: pass: pass-copy. Rubric question: 'Does any section heading merely rephrase its own first sentence with no added fact/number/mechanism, or is the same explanatory point repeated across two or more sections?' YES = warn. A heading that just names its topic ('Architecture' over an architecture diagram) is fine — flag same information content restated, not a shared topic word.
checks.fixture_violation: Heading 'Faster deploys' followed by 'Deploys are now faster.' with no new specifics.
checks.fp_guards: A topic sentence that states the claim AND immediately substantiates it with a number/mechanism passes. Only zero-added-specificity restatement or cross-section duplication fires.

checks.title: Load-bearing jargon never defined for the intended audience
checks.applies_when: Page is an explainer aimed at a broader/onboarding audience (not an internal team-only reference).
checks.spec: pass: pass-copy. Rubric question: 'Does the document rely on 2+ acronyms or internal system names that are load-bearing for understanding the diagram yet never expanded on first use (no parenthetical, no glossary, no title attribute) anywhere?' YES = warn.
checks.fixture_violation: An explainer using 'the CRDT merges via the WAL' with neither term ever expanded.
checks.fp_guards: Terms the explicit target audience already knows (an internal architecture doc for the team that coined them) are exempt — only flag when the artifact reads as onboarding/broad audience or the term is never defined anywhere.

checks.title: High-precision AI-slop phrases in rendered prose
checks.applies_when: Page has prose text nodes (headlines, leads, callouts, card descriptions, captions, slide body copy).
checks.spec: Extract rendered prose text nodes using the same exclusion filter as unslop-prose-phrases (exclude code/pre, identifiers, filenames, table headers, ALL-CAPS mono labels/kickers, counters, timestamps, status strings, Mermaid labels, and [BRACKET] system messages). Case-insensitively grep for any curated literal phrase or structural regex below — each is essentially impossible in legitimate technical-explainer prose. Any hit = warn. LITERALS: here's the thing:; let that sink in; make no mistake; read that again; this cannot be overstated; in today's fast-paced; in today's rapidly evolving; needless to say; it goes without saying; it is important to note that; it's important to note; pro tip:; hot take:; unpopular opinion:; plot twist:; spoiler:; stands as a testament; a testament to; pivotal moment; indelible mark; rich tapestry; cornerstone of; sends a clear message; nestled in the heart of; boasts a world-class; a hidden gem; a beacon of; at the forefront of; i hope this helps; certainly!; great question; happy to help; let me know if you need anything else; i'd be happy to; as an ai language model; i assure you; here's what's interesting; here's what caught my eye; here's what stood out; let me think step by step; here's my thought process; to answer your question; as of my knowledge cutoff; as of my last update; based on my training data; the future looks bright; exciting times lie ahead; only time will tell; one thing is certain; poised for growth; warrants further investigation; holds great promise; spanning everything from; double-edged sword; at the intersection of; the elephant in the room; it begs the question; buckle up; food for thought; let's dive in; let's unpack. REGEXES (case-insensitive): not only .{0,80}but also | it'?s not just about .+?,\s*it'?s about | \bit'?s not just .+?,?\s*it'?s\s | \bthe (answer|secret|key|trick|truth|reality|problem|solution|takeaway|lesson|difference|reason) (is|was|isn'?t|remains)\s*:. Deliberately EXCLUDED as too plausible in real explainer prose: bare 'robust'/'seamless'/'powerful'/'comprehensive'/'leverage'/'delve'/'in other words'/'at its core'/'when it comes to'/'the key takeaway:'/'it's worth noting'/'at the end of the day', and the bare nested-em-dash regex (too FP-prone for a static grep — left to the unslop-prose-style llm pass).
checks.fixture_violation: A lead paragraph reading 'In today's fast-paced landscape, this architecture stands as a testament to scalability.'
checks.fp_guards: Never scan code, labels, table headers, Mermaid labels, or [BRACKET] messages. Literal quotes/illustrative bad examples inside <blockquote>/<code> are exempt. Load-bearing technical words (leverage/navigate/harness in their literal sense) are absent from the list by design; single weak words are excluded so this stays high-precision. Overlaps with unslop-prose-phrases are resolved by the modified_checks entry (that check keeps only the however/moreover frequency heuristic).
checks.impl_hint: Case-insensitive substring/regex scan over the excluded-filtered prose text; report the matched phrase + surrounding sentence.
